## **1.1 Welcome Home \- So, Why This Issue, Why This Course?**

   
Welcome\! I'm Gyasi, and I'll be your guide and navigator as you embark on this transformative Masterclass.  
   
This course has been a labor of love between a colleague and I, developed over the last 15 years. 

Our journey together has been one of mutual support, as we've strived to become the best versions of ourselves.  
   
It all began with recognizing the undeniable power of human connection.  
   
You see Throughout my life, I've been fortunate enough to have incredible men and women support me in my pursuit of personal growth and success, and in return have made it my life’s work to reciprocate.  
   
Together we have been there to witness and participate in each other's transformations, providing gentle reminders of our true nature during challenging times.  
   
The compassion and empathy shown in these moments have left an everlasting impression.  
   
During my journey, I've been privileged to learn from diverse experiences and wisdom sources. 

I have traveled extensively, immersing myself in different cultures, exploring ancient traditions and practices, and drawing inspiration from the stories of those I've met.  
   
I've also delved into the world of science, learning about cutting-edge research on neuroplasticity, mindfulness, and behavioral change, which have all informed my approach to personal development.  
   
So, why create this course?  
   
Well, over the past two decades, I've worked with countless individuals from all walks of life, helping them overcome obstacles and barriers.   
   
I've supported men and women battling crippling addictions, struggling with severe mental health issues, or simply trying to find their footing.  

My experiences have taught me the importance of resilience, self-compassion, and the power of community in overcoming life's challenges.  
   
While Reflecting on MY journey, I acknowledged my commitment to becoming a man I could be proud of.  
   
I've been a part of men's groups and communities, walked spiritual paths, studied psychology and social work, and led workshops and teams focused on empowering others.  
   
These experiences have instilled a deep understanding of the importance of vulnerability, open communication, and empathy in personal growth.  
   
A significant turning point for me came with the birth of my son three years ago.  
   
Looking into his innocent eyes, I was filled with love and joy, yet also confronted with anxiety and unease. 

I couldn't help but wonder what kind of world my son would inherit, what kind of man he would become, and how society, culture, and the prevailing ethos would shape his values and self-development.   
   
As I considered the challenges my son might face in HIS life, I began to attune deeper to the barriers that we men often encounter in our relationships with ourselves and our sexuality. 

These barriers, like chains around our necks, can hold us down from reaching our full heights and true potential.  
   
Simultaneously in my practice, I quickly found the common theme of excessive pornography consumption as one of the most insidious modern-day epidemics affecting men.  
   
It disconnects us from our emotions, our sexual energy, our inner power, and our beautiful masculinity, leaving us adrift in a sea of endless content.  
   
It was my desire for not only the young man I am raising, but ALL men to have the freedom to make their own choices and become their versions of unbridled greatness, that inspired the creation of this course.  
   
I am here to join you as a brother-in-arms, an educator, and a guide on this journey towards freedom and self-discovery. 

Together, we will strive to become the best versions of ourselves, unlocking our inner Vitruvian man.  
   
My intention, is this course will help you break free from the chains of excessive porn watching AND empower you to create a more authentic and fulfilling life.  
   
By confronting and overcoming these challenges, you will reconnect with your true self, embrace your inner power, and step into your unique role in the world. The man you were destined to become\!  
   
And I want to express my deepest gratitude for your trust and commitment to this journey. 

I am honored to serve as your guide and mentor, and I look forward to witnessing your growth and transformation.  
   
Together, let’s embark on this path toward freedom, self-discovery, and the realization of our full potential as the Vitruvian men we are meant to be.  
   
So, let's begin this journey, with open hearts and minds, ready to embrace the challenges and triumphs that lie ahead.  
   
With every step, we will create a better future for ourselves, our loved ones, and the world around us.  
   
Are you ready to break free and become the best version of yourself?  
   
BRAVO\!\!\!  
   
Join me, and let's get started.

---

 

## **1.2 The Vitruvian Man** 

   
Hello, my friend. Before we begin, I want to congratulate you on the choice you have made.   
   
By both purchasing this course, and choosing to invest your time into making change in your life, you have chosen the Vitruvian Man within you, over the lesser version of yourself.  
   
Not everyone chooses to pursue their higher self. Really, take this in……It takes wisdom, courage, and self respect to make that decision, and I want to formally recognize that.   
   
More importantly, I want YOU to recognize that. You deserve to be commended for this effort.   
   
The Vitruvian Man is a world-renowned drawing created by the Italian polymath Leonardo da Vinci around 1490\. (Insert Drawing here)  
   
As you can see, it portrays a male figure with outstretched limbs, superimposed in two different positions within a circle and a square, and is based on the architectural and anatomical theories of the ancient Roman architect Vitruvius.  
   
By merging geometry and human anatomy, the Vitruvian Man represents the Renaissance concept of the microcosm and macrocosm, emphasizing the belief that the human body is a microcosm reflecting the structure and order of the larger cosmos. 

To put it simply…. You come from the same material as the stars in the universe and hold the same potential.  
   
I’ve entitled my company Vitruvian Man, because this represents to me, the pursuit of the Higher Self, which is in perfect harmony and alignment with its surroundings.  
   
The Vitruvian Man is an ideal. But make no mistake, it is an obtainable ideal.  
   
It’s not about controlling the outer world at all\! but about commanding what we can control \- our internal world, which in turn puts us in tune with the outside world \- opening our powers of manifestation.   
   
This concept is not new, there have been countless books, writings, and films about this paradigm, I am sure you have come across it before. 

If this is your first-time hearing about it, don’t worry you will have a chance to dive deeper into it through the resources in this course to further your understanding.  
   
So, what is this course about?  
   
Well, Pornography addiction is just one way in which a man, in this case, internally, can be out of alignment with his world.  
   
I’m sure you have already come to this realization, evidenced by your purchase of this course. 

You can no longer deny the compulsion toward pornographic images warps you away from your true proportions, and it causes real PAIN in your life.  
   
This course is designed to allow you to see yourself, to see exactly how this habit has managed to get a hold of you and contort you into what you know you are NOT.  
   
And once you can see clearly, without the vail, haze, and distortions it will all make sense. And YOU will set yourself free from porn addiction.   
   
Quitting porn is not difficult. It’s actually incredibly easy. You probably do not believe it or have doubts, but once you absorb the information in this course, you WILL.  
   
It WILL be easy, it WILL be painless, and it WILL even be.. dare I say…enjoyable. 

This method has helped millions of people quit their addictions, and I have witnessed it in my professional and personal circles over and over again.     
   
Let’s take a step back now and be sure to clear up what this course is NOT \-  
   
This course is not a list or description of all the bad things about porn. You already know the negative outcomes of porn consumption, that is why you’re here.  
   
I will not attempt to shock you with stories of how people's lives have been ruined and essentially seek to scare you into quitting. 

I don’t believe those methods work for everyone, nor offer the complete freedom the modern man is looking for.  
   
I often hear from men with addictions how those methods are condescending, out of touch with their present reality of who they are, demand more of them that they are not capable of, and thus have little impact on their ability to quit.  
   
This course is not about shaming you. It’s the opposite of that.  
This course will show you just how NOT broken you are.  
   
There is nothing to be ashamed of. You are just like everyone else, and I will show you that, and you will experience it for yourself.  
   
I just want to add one detail here for you to think about….  
The power you have to contort yourself out of proportion… is also the power you have to align yourself perfectly with the true Self that you know and feel deeply, or have simply disconnected from..  
   
So do not look for shame in this course my friend. Trust me when I say you will not find it here.   
   
In short, This course is simple, yet effective.  
And it has helped countless people transform themselves.  
   
There are only 4 simple requirements that I ask for those who take this course. These requirements are deliberate and designed for your maximum benefit. So please take note and abide by these rules:   
   
   
One \- Do not quit porn while you are taking this course. Continue your habit or routine until the very end. Attempting to quit before the end could have negative consequences on your progress. 

If you have already started a challenge or have some time under your belt then that’s great, continue as you are going along and follow the course as instructed as if you were still actively watching porn.  
   
Two, – Do not skip ahead to any lessons or modules. Complete them sequentially along with the meditations and journal prompts. 

This ensures that you get the full impact of the course which has been designed in a very specific and deliberate way to deliver the best results.   
   
Three \- Probable one of the most important pieces to this journey  
Keep an open mind. You do not HAVE to take anything that I say as gospel. 

By the end of this course, you can choose to do whatever you want, so just keep an open mind to everything I have to say and consider my words carefully.  
   
Four \-  Complete the full course. Do not quit the course. Finish.   
   
That is all. Only 4 requisites:  
Don’t quit porn while you take the course,  
Don't skip ahead,  
Keep an open mind,  
and finish the entire course.  
   
If you follow all of these directions, you will walk away from porn, it will be easy for you, and you will be ecstatic about it.   
   
Alright, then, let’s wrap up this module with your first contemplative meditation and journal prompts.  
   
These are essential pieces of the process.  
You might be unfamiliar with meditating or journaling, but please trust me when I say these should not be skipped either.  
   
So, when you’re ready, please download the journal prompts from Module 1 and then I’ll meet you in our Guided Meditation.

## **1.3 A Guided Meditation**

   
Welcome to this guided meditation.   
   
Find a comfortable position, either sitting or lying down. Close your eyes and take a few deep breaths, inhaling through your nose and exhaling through your mouth.  
   
Feel your body relax and your mind become more focused as you breathe.  
   
Visualize yourself standing at the edge of a peaceful forest. The sun is shining brightly, and you can feel its warmth on your skin. As you take a step into the forest, you feel a sense of curiosity and excitement about the journey ahead.  
   
As you walk deeper into the forest, you notice that the trees begin to clear, revealing a wide and inviting path.  
   
This path represents the journey you are about to embark on, a journey towards freedom and self-discovery. With each step you take, your excitement grows, and your anxieties and hesitations begin to fade away.  
   
It is natural for thoughts and feelings to arise during meditation.  
   
If you notice any discomfort or distractions, gently acknowledge them and bring your focus back to the path before you. Remember, this is a journey of self-exploration, and every experience is an opportunity for growth.  
   
As you continue to walk, you encounter a gentle stream, its waters clear and refreshing.  
   
This stream represents the flow of positive energy and the washing away of any fears or doubts that have been holding you back. Pause for a moment and take a deep breath, allowing this positive energy to fill your entire being.  
   
As you cross the stream and continue on your path, you notice that the forest becomes brighter and more vibrant. You feel a renewed sense of determination and commitment to your journey, and any remaining barriers or hesitations are swept away by the warmth of the sun.  
   
Now, imagine yourself standing at the end of the path, having successfully completed the course and overcome your excessive porn use.  
   
Feel the pride, relief, and joy that comes with this accomplishment. Take a deep breath and allow these feelings to wash over you, reinforcing your motivation and excitement for the journey ahead.  
   
As we begin to bring this meditation to a close, take a few more deep breaths.   
   
Remember that it is normal for thoughts and feelings to arise during meditation, and it is important to be patient and gentle with yourself as you navigate this journey.  
   
When you are ready, slowly open your eyes and carry the sense of calm and openness you've cultivated throughout the meditation with you as you move forward.

**Module Two: The online porn industry**  
   
**2.1 Everything is about sex, Except sex.**  
   
Heyooo Welcome\!  
So, before jumping into how we address our problems with porn, let's first take a step back and look at the bigger picture.  
   
Pornography exists within the context of an even larger, and pervasive cultural phenomenon, which is the hyper-sexualization of media and society. This is the world we live in\!  
   
Take a moment to think about it \- It makes perfect sense; Sex is innate and fundamental to us \- it’s biologically hardwired to have a profound significance in our experiences, because it’s the basis of how we procreate and persevere as a species.   
   
Now what do you think happens when you take maybe the strongest biological impulse that we have, and you place it in the context of a free-market driven economy?  
   
That fundamental biological impulse becomes one of the most powerful marketing mechanisms ever deployed\! 

It’s woven into practically every media message you receive.  
   
You’ve heard the expression “Sex Sells”, right?  
With that said, \- there might not be anything on earth that sells products better than sex\!  
   
We live in a sea of sexual suggestion \- the ads for the clothes we buy, the tv shows we watch with hot steamy sex scenes, the commercials for the soda we like to drink and the car we want to drive.  

You know what I’m talking about \- the car ad with the hot girl in the skimpy outfit mesmerized by the guy who pulls up in the brand new $80,000 car.  

It’s not even hidden, it's unapologetically right in our faces.    
   
Sure, they’re selling us a product, but they’re also offering sex, subliminally.  
   
It’s virtually inescapable.   
In fact \- A survey by the American Academy of Pediatrics found that 56% of TV commercials during prime-time programming contained sexual content.  
   
You see the thing about sex – is it's not only a nero-biological compulsion, but it’s also an empty glass that we fill with meaning.  
   
Now, What do I mean by that?  
   
There’s a saying: “Everything is about sex, except for sex”.  
Think about it.  
   
Especially as men, (but women also), so much of our lives and choices are spent indirectly pursuing, or optimizing what will make us sexually attractive.  
   
The job we want, the car we want, the clothes we want, the status we want.  
   
We’re always thinking and doing what we hope will make us more attractive as mates.  
   
Yet, when we actually attain SEX, and we’re in the act of DOING IT, in our minds, we are pursuing other things – reinforced by our ego’s.  
   
We want someone to love us, and to stay with us.  
We have aggression we want to express, we have sadness we want to avoid.  
   
We’re thinking about everything else aside from the man or woman right in front of us. Our future, our past, boosting our confidence, or we might even be internally degrading ourselves.  
   
So often when we’re having sex (including masturbation), we are not present.   
   
And so sex becomes something that we mindlessly pursue, and then when we get it, we make it into something else entirely.  
   
That’s also what makes it such an effective marketing vehicle. 

When they sell me that car, and insinuate I’ll get sex with the beautiful girl \- what I’m REALLY buying is the self-esteem, ego, and status that I believe I will get after having HAD sex with that beautiful girl.  
   
Does that make sense?  
You see \- Most of our sexual experiences take place in our mind \- and the mind is super powerful.  
   
So, If sexualized messaging is the “Kool-Aid” that we’re all drinking AND have come to accept without question, then internet porn is the equivalent of snorting undiluted, pure, Kool-Aid powder.  
   
It’s sex concentrate.  
   
Which leads us down the path to wanting more \- so we seek it out in Porn.  
   
And Porn isn’t new \- it’s always been there, but over the past 30 years since the inception of the internet, the delivery of this drug has been ingeniously perfected.  
   
It's been consistently made into a more and more potent substance \- and, of course, SOLD directly to you \- The End “User”.  
   
Forget the tactics of selling you the car with the suggestion that you’ll eventually get sex \- just enter your credit card, and purchase it instantly, in whatever form you want.  
   
Bring yourself to climax immediately.

It’s insidious, and simultaneously ingenious.  
   
**You could not come up with a more potent, instantly delivered, completely private, addictive, cheap drug than internet porn**.  
   
The combination of the internet, your sexual neural biology, and the free-market have provided the ingredients for the perfect laboratory, where this new Super Drug has been synthesized.  
   
And it's not just you, my friend, MILLIONS ARE HOOKED\!  
   
Okay, that was a lot to take in \- you're doing great\!  
Take a deep breath and when you're ready I will catch you in the following video.

**2.2 Dirty Money**  
   
Heyyoo- welcome back, let’s review a few stats about the porn industry.  
   
It’s incredible to me – I mean guys porn has been around for a millennia\!  
   
27,000 years ago, stone sculptures depicted women with large and exaggerated breasts and behinds. (Insert Image here)  
   
In fact, “porn” has been at the forefront of advancing almost every new form of media innovation \- from sculptures to the printing press, to vhs, and the internet.  
   
In 1995, the first company to do real-time credit card verifications online was (you guessed it) an internet-porn business. AND they were transacting over $1.5b a year in revenue\!  
   
This innovation created the foundation for what is today's 10 Trillion dollar e-commerce industry.  
   
And not only does technology continuously progress forward \- but the rate of change INCREASES as it moves forward.  It’s exponential.   
   
I hear often from clients born in the last generation. How life was so different a mere 20 years ago. The fear that technology is outpacing their capabilities.   
How, forced they feel to stay connected and updated constantly or else they will be left behind or even worse, the old men in the room out of touch with reality. 

Dismissed by the younger generations.  
   
I can’t argue with the facts, it’s true, new versions and options emerge faster and faster.  
   
Hundreds of years used to separate the emergence of new technology.  
   
Nowadays some new life altering technology emerges every few years \- its insane.   
   
Now, it’s helpful to understand, certain scientific discoveries and technologies are massive accelerants to our world: the steam engine, electricity, computing, and antibiotics, just to name a few.  
   
Yet with the discovery or creation of each one of these, an entire era of world-changing innovations followed. 

And perhaps one of the most impactful that is relevant for our work here, has been the internet.   
   
Of course, this fantastic technology has brought us the ability to exchange information, ideas and money \- in the blink of an eye.  
   
It has almost instantaneously shrunk our entire world to one global village, where anyone can access almost anything. Including, what we crave the most \- SEX.  
   
And boy have we hit the throttle without a second thought..   
   
With that said, did you know  
   
That Porn sites receive more daily visitors than Netflix?  
   
YEP\! Every second, 28,000 people are viewing porn on the internet.  
– In 2019 1.3 million hours of porn were uploaded to Pornhub  
Also \- 20% of men and 13% of women admit to watching porn at work.   
   
And Those are just a few stats to demonstrate just how pervasive porn is\!  
   
There are a few more for you to check out in the resource section below this lesson..   
   
This wave of technology-fueled binging on sexual content is unprecedented, socially, economically, biologically, and neurologically.  
   
It’s true \- a young man can consume more images of beautiful naked women in one day, than a King could 400 years ago.  
   
As a matter of fact, it’s hard to imagine that the human brain is even designed to be able to manage this type of stimuli without serious repercussions.  
   
As Kanye West put it: “no one man should have all that power”.  
    
Guys, this is the world we live in currently, just know we are dealing with the phenomenon of unlimited access to an extremely powerful “substance” at the scale of the entire human population.  
   
The numbers I’ve shared with you indicate massive swaths of people becoming hooked or addicted.  
   
Still, given the nature of the content, it’s almost certainly the case that the self-reported numbers are vastly understated.   
   
And all of this is to say, You are not alone. YOU are NOT abnormal.  
   
This is super important for you to realize, as a tremendous amount of shame is usually associated with consuming pornographic content.  
   
And this shame exists ONLY because everyone is pretending they're not doing it.  
   
This unwillingness to talk about it is one of the reasons that porn addiction is so insidious. Every human on this earth has sexual impulses \- we respond to sexual images, suggestions, and the like.  
   
So, it’s only natural if we were to be targeted by an unending stream of sexual stimuli – we would become hooked on it.  
   
Millions and millions of folks \- both men and women are beginning to emerge with stories of being addicted to porn and having their lives impacted in devastating ways because of it. 

Just take a look at the number of subscribers in porn addiction reddit groups. I have and it's astounding.   
   
It’s truly a global, mental health crisis, whether people would like to call it that or not. 

It's time that we acknowledge that fact, address it, so we can move beyond it to lead happy and fulfilled lives.   
   
Does that make sense? If not that’s fine too. You don’t have to either believe nor accept what I am saying. 

I often tell my clients it is more important to keep an open mind as instructed earlier. If you are having doubts, just look around and ask yourself. 

Are most of the people I know happy with their sex lives? 

When was the last time I heard about someone telling me of a gratifying porn watching session. 

And If I knew what I was getting into when I started viewing porn, would I have started watching it in the first place? I suspect you know the answer.  
   
We’ll take a break here, you are doing great. Just keep on moving forward step by step and I’ll see you in our next lesson\!

**2.3 The Mental Manipulation**  
   
Heyoo welcome back \- Now it’s time to be candid about brainwashing and addiction.  
   
But before we navigate into that topic, I need to be crystal clear about something guys \- I am NOT “anti-pornography”, and neither is this course.   
   
Remember, this Course is about self awareness, and empowering YOU to make the best choices you can possibly make for both yourself and your family \- regardless of whatever ingeniously devised temptations are dangled before your eyes.  
   
This course is about unraveling how we got here and what we believe is keeping us from making new choices.  
   
Time and time again I hear from clients their wish to transform their lives but feel trapped in quicksand. 

When they start to understand and feel their power there simply isn’t anymore quicksand, only stepping stones which they use to move further and further towards their true longing for joy and pleasure.  
   
One of the first steps in understanding what is happening to you is to recognize what has been happening to the society you live in, and how the things that you consume have evolved, and how they affect you. 

Simply put a deeper awareness of the sandbox you play in.  
   
Since as far back as you can remember, well into your childhood, you have been inundated with sexual suggestions, which have been gradually and unyieldingly ramped up more and more, all the way to adulthood.  
   
Most of the decisions you’ve made, from the mundane to the profound, have been influenced by your notions of sex and sexuality, and many of those notions were fed to you through all the forms of media you consume \- TV, Music, Internet, and Social media.  
   
AND \- The ideas you were fed, were also fed to your friends and peers.  
   
We believe the messages we hear and THEN we hear those same beliefs repeated amongst our friends, deepening that belief into the core of our being.  
   
So as we move through life, we internalize these beliefs, solidifying them into our worldviews, identities, and expectations of the outer world.  
   
And make no mistake, these beliefs subconsciously direct our decisions and actions, thus putting much of our sex lives on “auto-pilot”, while we sit in the back seat. Thinking we are actually finding pleasure from the nauseating twists and turns.  
   
Can you see it now?  
When it comes to sex, we’ve been brainwashed, and when it comes to porn, we’ve become addicted.  
   
We are brainwashed and Addicted.   
   
But look, here’s the good news: Un-brainwashing and Un-addicting ourselves from pornography \- in reality \- are not difficult things to do.  
   
They simply require a new perspective.  
   
\*\*  
Society may have initially influenced you in that first tank of sub premium gas for your ride, But it’s actually YOU who keeps refilling your tank now, and keeping YOU out of the driver seat, while your compulsions fishtail your car down the highway.  
   
Once you understand that, you’ll know you can quit.  
   
We have to SEE the con, as to not get played.  
   
It may seem too challenging  
And Maybe you’ve even tried to quit in the past and failed.  
   
But If you follow all the steps in this course, you WILL gain that new perspective, and in doing so WILL gain a new power of choice in your life. 

Take a breath, it’s can be a lot and overwhelming, yet at the same time give yourself a pat on the back, or a little flex for being in the right place at this moment.  
   
Excellent work, let’s keep the ball rolling and move on to the following video.

Parents, friends, peers, media, society, TV, music, all fed us with expectations about what should drive us and who we’re supposed to be.

**2.5 A Reflective Meditation**   
Welcome to this meditation focused on feeling your body and developing an intimacy with yourself. This meditation will help you foster a sense of self-love, acceptance, and connection to your body.  
   
Find a comfortable position, either seated or lying down, and let's begin.  
   
Lets start by preparation and grounding: Close your eyes gently and take a few deep breaths. Inhale deeply through your nose, allowing your lungs to fill with air, and exhale slowly through your mouth. Feel your body start to relax, letting go of any tension or tightness.  
   
Bring your awareness to the points of contact between your body and the surface you're resting on. Feel the support beneath you and let it ground you in this present moment.  
   
Next we will scan the body: Bring your attention to the top of your head. Imagine a warm, soothing light gently washing over you, starting at the crown of your head. As it moves downward, allow it to guide your attention and awareness through each part of your body.  
   
Acknowledge the sensations: As you scan your body, notice any sensations that arise. They may be pleasant, uncomfortable, or neutral. Observe them without judgment, simply allowing them to be present.  
   
When you encounter a sensation, pause for a moment to fully acknowledge it. Breathe into it, sending love and acceptance to that part of your body. Remember that each sensation is a reminder of your body's presence and the intimate connection you have with it.  
   
Connecting deeper with your breath by shifting your attention to the breath. Feel the rise and fall of your chest and abdomen as you breathe in and out. Notice the sensation of the air entering and leaving your nostrils, and the way it flows through your body.  
   
Allow your breath to anchor you, deepening your connection to your body and your sense of intimacy with yourself.  
   
Next embrace self-love by bringing your hands to your heart center or another part of your body that feels meaningful to you. As you touch your body, express gratitude for all it does for you, and acknowledge the love and appreciation you have for yourself.  
   
Take a moment to repeat the following affirmations silently or out loud, connecting with their meaning:

·             	"I am deeply connected to my body."  
·             	"I am grateful for my body and its abilities."  
·             	"I love and accept myself unconditionally."  
   
Release tension by gently release your hands and take a few more deep breaths. If you encounter any remaining tension, imagine it dissolving away with each exhale, leaving your body completely relaxed and at peace.  
   
Gradually bring your awareness back to the room around you. Gently wiggle your fingers and toes, awakening your body. Take a deep, rejuvenating breath, and as you exhale, slowly open your eyes.  
   
Thank yourself for taking the time to cultivate self-awareness, love, and intimacy with your body. Carry this renewed connection and sense of appreciation with you throughout your day or into your evening.

**Module Three: Your Brain and Body on Porn**  
**Outline**  
   
3.1 You Are an Addict  
3.2 The Vicious Cycle  
3.3 What’s So Great About Porn?  
3.4 The Trap  
3.5 Our Recap   
3.6 Homework & A Guided Meditation 

**Module Three: Your Brain and Body on Porn**  
 

## **3.1 You are an addict.**

##  

Heyoo welcome back\! I salute you for your commitment and dedication to this process.  
   
Let’s pick up where we left off.  
   
So, we’ve established that we swim in a sea of sexual suggestion, imagery, and the constant marketing of sex.  
   
Yet, there is a spectrum of how explicit and intense all of these sexual stimuli are, and porn is by far at the extreme end of that spectrum.  
   
It’s only natural and most likely unavoidable that we will eventually find our way to viewing pornography and getting a taste of what it’s like. 

Culture, society, curiosity, and our own sexual urges LEAD us to porn, but what “hooks” us on consuming porn?   
   
What is it that makes us come back, again and again??   
Why is it SOOO appealing?  
   
After all, its not even REAL sex. It's just pictures and images.  
   
You don’t even get to actually FEEL what you’re watching. You’re not ACTUALLY with another person. You’re by yourself.  
   
Still, we think it’s so great \- why?? Have you asked yourself this question?  
   
The answer is VERY simple.  
You are addicted to it.  
   
And I don’t mean that as a figure of speech. I mean that you are addicted to porn, the EXACT same way that a cocaine addict is addicted to cocaine.  
   
I mean this VERY literally.  
You are CHEMICALLY addicted to porn and that is why you keep coming back.   
   
Picture this: a scientist places two seemingly unrelated substances on a table – a small bag of cocaine and a smartphone with a pornographic video playing. Insert Image  
   
At first glance, you might think these two items may appear to have nothing in common. However, when we delve into the neurochemistry of the human brain, it becomes apparent that there is far less difference between the two, than you might imagine.   
   
\*\*\*Studies show that consuming pornography is neuro-chemically indistinguishable from consuming hard drugs like cocaine. 

To understand this, we need to understand how “addiction” works in the brain. 

So, let's dive into the world of neurotransmitters, dopamine, and addiction. You don’t need an advanced degree to understand this, and it’s quite basic and if you are interested in diving deeper into this, there are links for resources that accompany this module.  
   
Dopamine, aaaah yes sweet dopamine, a neurotransmitter, that plays a vital role in the brain's reward and pleasure systems. 

It is released in response to rewarding experiences, such as eating delicious food, exercising, or engaging in sexual activity, or consuming other “pleasurable” stimuli.  
   
The release of dopamine is essential for maintaining motivation and reinforcing behaviors that promote survival and well-being.  
   
Pornography triggers the release of dopamine, as the brain perceives it as a novel and rewarding sexual experience.  
   
When we consume pornographic material, the surge of dopamine creates a pleasurable sensation.  
   
But then… afterwards, the dopamine DROPS precipitously, causing a “craving”.  
   
The initial pleasure, followed by a drop, serves to reinforce the behavior and encourages us to seek out more porn. 

In essence, the brain becomes wired to seek out the quick and easy dopamine rush provided by pornography, leading to the development of addiction.  
   
For our purposes I have simplified it considerably however, this process is practically IDENTICAL to how someone becomes addicted to cocaine.  
   
Cocaine causes a surge or build-up of dopamine in the brain, leading to sensations of euphoria and intense “pleasure”. And then, once the dopamine levels subside, they fall back below their normal baseline levels \- triggering the experience of a ‘CRAVING”.  
   
The cocaine addict HAS to go find some more cocaine, or they will be extremely unhappy, to put it mildly.  
   
We feel that same craving for porn.  
There is only one chemical, and one process primarily responsible for all forms of addiction \- Dopamine and the pleasure/reward system that governs its release.  
   
And physical substances are not the ONLY things that can trigger the dopaminergic response. ANYTHING that your brain has deemed is pleasurable, can trigger dopamine to start its cycle.   
   
In my work with clients in recovery understanding this base level of brain chemistry is important. 

The experience of addiction can appear as overwhelming, uncontrollable and at some level disconnected from the body.  
   
Knowing what is happening as a first step has given insight into the internal workings, allowing clients to pay closer attention to what is happening in real time.  
   
This is a significant step in moving past the feelings of helplessness so often associated with addiction.  
   
Fascinating, isn’t it??  
Alright, let’s keep going and I will see you in the following video.  
---

 

## **3.2 The Vicious Cycle**

   
Heyoo Welcome Back\!

In this lesson let’s continue our dopaminergic conversation and also review the cycle we go through – namely Escalation – Desensitization – and then the loss of intimacy.  
   
The dopaminergic response doesn’t just end with you wanting more and more.  
   
The cycle of seeking out surging levels of dopamine, and then plunging them back below their normal levels also leads to Escalation and Desensitization.  
   
You see, our brain is highly malleable.

It learns, adapts, and molds to our behaviors and the stimuli we give it. When we continue to consume, we enter into a cycle of “escalation” and “desensitization”.   
   
The escalation of pornography consumption refers to this progressive increase in the frequency, duration, or extremity of the material viewed to achieve the same level of arousal or satisfaction.  
   
Over time, as an individual continues to consume pornography, the brain becomes desensitized to the dopamine release, requiring more significant or more novel stimuli to achieve the same pleasurable response.  
   
This phenomenon, known as tolerance, is similar to what occurs with drug addiction, wherein higher doses or more potent substances are needed to produce the desired effect.   
   
We clearly see to maintain the same level of satisfaction, we may increase the frequency or duration of our pornography consumption or seek out more extreme or novel content, such as unconventional fetishes, acts of violence, or taboo scenarios.  
   
This is what's called Desensitization.   
Desensitization is the other side of the escalation coin.  
   
As we search for more and more intense stimuli, it becomes more and more difficult to be aroused in general.  
   
And we’ve all experienced this in some form.  
   
Maybe the first bit of porn we ever saw were bare breasts in a PlayBoy magazine.

Seeing that blew our fucking tops off\!

But today? Not even close.  
   
Think about the first porn you watched. It was probably tame in comparison to what you are watching now. 

I often hear “I can’t believe I am watching porn that is degrading, violent, and at times borderline illegal.  

Or I went from simple pictures to shocking 3d virtual immersive experiences. I need more and more to get me going.  
   
Through continued exposure to sexual stimuli, the novelty just wears off. 

We have progressively gotten desensitized to sexual images. But porn \- especially easily accessible, online porn, accelerates that process dramatically.  
   
We can very quickly find ourselves endlessly scrolling through thousands of images and videos of all categories, looking for something that will excite us.   
   
Sadly though, if we take a step back, we see this diminished ability to be aroused, extending out into the real world with our partners. 

One of the immediate and most common “negative” impacts that people experience as a result of too much porn use, is lack of arousal with their own sexual partner.  
   
Why?  
Well remember, we’ve got to think of online porn as a synthetic substance.  
   
This substance I call Sex Concentrate,  
and it's injected directly into the pleasure centers of your brain via your eyeballs and manual stimulation when you masturbate.  
   
The reality is that, given the way brain chemistry works, the real world just CANNOT cause that type of constant and extreme stimulation.  
   
Unfortunately, this will quickly bring a man or woman to find their partner “boring”. And this lack of arousal between partners leads to deeper problems, as intimacy between the two fades.   
   
Sex, intimacy, and arousal are distinct but interconnected aspects of human relationships.  
   
By sex we are referring to the physical act of engaging in sexual activity with another person, while intimacy encompasses the emotional closeness, vulnerability, and trust that form the foundation of a deep and meaningful relationship.  
   
Arousal, on the other hand, is the physiological and psychological response to sexual stimuli, which can be triggered by various factors, including physical touch, visual cues, and emotional connection.  
   
In healthy relationships, sex, intimacy, and arousal work in harmony to create a fulfilling and mutually satisfying sexual experience.  
   
A strong emotional bond enhances sexual desire and arousal, while satisfying sex can strengthen the emotional connection between partners.  
   
However, when an external factor like pornography enters into the equation, the delicate balance between these elements can be disrupted, leading to a decline in overall relationship satisfaction.  
   
Depending on where you are with your porn consumption you might have already seen these effects or started to feel something is just off. 

Considering that you are here I would say for sure you have come to that conclusion already, and good for you\!  
   
One of the primary issues with overconsumption of pornography is its isolating effect. 

Watching porn is typically a solitary activity, which means that it inherently lacks the emotional connection and interpersonal dynamics that are present during sex with a partner.  
   
As we become more reliant on pornography for arousal and sexual gratification, we may begin to view sex as a purely physical act, devoid of the emotional intimacy that is so crucial for fostering deep connections with a partner.   
   
Sadly, This social withdrawal and isolation ultimately reinforces the porn addiction by continuing to keep you isolated and disconnected.   
   
Just as with drug abuse, users eventually develop a relationship with the substance. 

That relationship becomes more and more consuming, then more and more important, and then ultimately  becomes their PRIMARY relationship.  
   
A heroin addict will give up almost anything else in their life, no matter what it is \- even their loved ones, to stay in a relationship with their substance.

And Porn addiction can be just as insidious, as you may already know.  
   
I listen to stories of men who sacrifice quality time with their children, friends, partners, pretend to stay late at work, or lie about how busy they are just to keep themselves “available” to watch porn. Honestly, it's touching and sad.  
   
You see, the deeper we go into porn, the further away from our real-life relationships we get. Our new “significant other” becomes the porn on our computers, or the only fans women we subscribe to, or our stash of favorite sites or vids.   
   
With that said, it’s essential to view our behavior and interaction with porn in this light. 

If you don’t agree that’s fine too, you don’t need to agree, just keep an open mind and ask yourself is porn enhancing my relationships or detracting from them.  
   
I want to pause for a moment and reiterate, comparing porn consumption to the behaviors of drug addicts is not about shaming our porn use \- in fact, quite the opposite. 

It’s about understanding the power of the mind and human physiology, so that we can eventually become masters of our own bodies, instead of being slaves to our chemical impulses.  
   
We CAN override these processes to not only STOP the behaviors that don't serve us but CREATE the behaviors that will build us the life we truly desire.   
   
Take a breath again, deeply so you can really let the material sink in.

Its a lot to digest, but you’ve got this and you’re doing amazing\!

Take a break, and when you’re ready I’ll catch you in our next lesson\!  
   
---

 

## **3.3 What’s so great about porn?**

   
Heyoo welcome, let's take a deep breath together and jump back in.  
   
We’ll start by discussing the “elephant in the room” You ready?  
   
Man to man lets look in the mirror and ask ourselves with complete honesty. What is SO great about porn?  
   
As you hopefully realize by now, this course is NOT about telling you all the bad things about porn.  
   
It’s not about listing the ways how it can be detrimental to your happiness, your relationships, or your health.  
   
You already know these things and that's why you’re here. It’s a given you’re already sick and tired of being on the hamster wheel.  
   
But that said, what is so GREAT about masturbating to pornography?  
   
First of all, let's separate two things \-  
Watching porn, and masturbation.  
   
These are two separate activities, often done together, are actually very distinctive experiences. I'll explain further.    
   
Masturbation is manually giving yourself sexual pleasure.  
   
When we watch porn, the goal is to activate mental arousal to go along with the manual sexual stimulation. Most times the goal is to achieve an orgasm.  
   
So, what is it we love so much about combining the two or masturbating to porn.   
   
Stay with me here…When you are masturbating to pornography, you are essentially “having sex” by yourself, while looking at someone else having sex.  
   
You’re alone, in a room, manually bringing yourself to sexual climax, while watching other people having ACTUAL SEX. 

Now, I’m not saying masturbation is WRONG, or that it can’t even be a healthy part of one's sexuality.  
   
But are we really going to say that it's THAT GREAT??  
That it IS desirable? That it IS better having sex alone under these conditions, than with a person?  
   
Is it really that satisfying to watch another man give and receive pleasure, instead of YOU being the one giving and receiving pleasure??  
   
Is it?  
   
Let’s pause for a moment to be honest with ourselves,  
   
If we look at it from the outside in, we would SEE that constantly having sex alone, to images of other people having real sex, is pretty sad.   
   
Deep down we know this, and we’re familiar with the feelings that accompany this behavior.   
   
Right?  
And ask yourself, what do you feel when you orgasm after watching porn online?  
Why do we immediately rush to close all the windows and shut the screen off when we’re done?  
   
It’s like snapping out of a spell… I hear it so often there is an immediate feeling of disgust, shame, and self-loathing. The images being viewed all of a sudden seem ridiculous or outrageous.  
   
Breathe I can feel the intensity rising.  
Let’s process those feelings \-  
Do you feel a sense of pride in that moment?  
Is this something you’re eager to tell a friend about?  
Do you feel more powerful, sexually, after masturbating to porn?  
   
Of course not. And it’s actually the opposite. It is very hard to have respect for oneself in those moments.   
   
So what do we get from it?  
An orgasm? 10 seconds of mindless pleasure?  
   
Frankly, that's a worse payoff than doing drugs \- at least with drugs the high lasts longer\! Now in no way am I advocating drug use but clearly it’s not really the orgasm we’re after, is it?  
   
The orgasm just lets us know we’re finished and it’s time to clean up.  
   
Okay, so if it’s not the orgasm we’re after \- then what?  
What do we hope to accomplish by scrolling through pages and pages of images for an hour or several hours hmmm?  
   
To answer that, let's back up one step…  
Let's back up to the step right before you begin watching.  
   
Were you actually that horny?? Were you even in a good mood?  
How often are you in a fucking GREAT mood and think to yourself “man I GOTTA go watch porn and jerk off\!”  
   
No, in fact I’d be willing to bet, it’s the opposite.  
   
You’re feeling low, stressed out, anxious, nervous, depressed with low exergy and THAT'S when you think to yourself “I want to go jerk off to porn”. Sound familiar?  
   
Remember “Everything’s about sex, except for sex”.  
   
You wanting to go watch porn is not about your sexual gratification \- it's about brainwashing, neuro-chemical dopamine addiction, and you having convinced yourself that this activity will alleviate your emotional discomfort we just described.    
   
And here’s the irony of it all \- Consuming porn is actually CAUSING you a tremendous amount of emotional discomfort, which you are then USING porn to medicate.   
   
Can you see what I am saying??  
   
Now, Let’s start the exploration as to how we’ve fallen into this trap.  
   
And with that I will see you in our next video.  
---

 

## **3.4 The Trap**

   
Heyoo  
Welcome back, you are moving along, and I commend you for that. You are still here\!  
   
One of the most common experiences I hear from men is that they feel trapped by their behavior and can’t seem to grasp how they got there. 

I often ask, and will encourage you here to keep in mind the ways you continue to brainwash yourself about your porn usage.  Suspend judgment and shame and ask.  
   
What are the things you tell yourself about WHY you LIKE to view porn?   
   
Generally, most of us believe that we get either relaxation, excitement, or sexual satisfaction FROM porn. 

These are the most common answers to this question that I hear consistently. 

But let’s take a closer look at each one, and see how much truth there actually is in these beliefs…   
 

First there’s Relaxation  
   
Listen to these statements expressed by users when asked about why they turn to porn.  
   
*“I come home from a long day and I just need some time for myself to relax”. Porn gives me time for just me to be with me”*  
   
or  
   
*“I’m in a high pressure environment at work and need to blow off some steam”. Porn is the easiest way to relieve this pressure*.  
   
or  
   
*I have so many responsibilities, a wife, kids, house payments, the list goes on, I feel constantly burdened and exhausted, porn allows me to escape these burdens for a brief moment.*  
   
Sound familiar?

The belief that porn provides relaxation is rooted in the initial sense of relief and pleasure that arises from the release of dopamine in the brain, as we learned about earlier.  

But look closer, is the activity itself relaxing? 

Hiding behind locked doors, scrolling through pages of endless content, focusing intently on extreme scenes and images, opening and closing browser windows, being vigilant that someone will catch you.  
   
Really?? That's RELAXING??  
   
And let me ask you this.  If it IS so relaxing, and you do it so often \- why aren’t you more of a relaxed person??

 If this activity is bringing so much relaxation into your life, why are you a nervous wreck? 

If you did something super relaxing every day of your life, you should be feeling pretty good most of the time\!   
   
We’re not finding relaxation binging on porn.  It’s just a lie we tell ourselves. It's the opposite.  

The truth is, paradoxically, while attempting to find relaxation through pornography, we are perpetuating a cycle that ultimately heightens our stress and discomfort.  
   
You become a more STRESSED OUT AND LESS RELAXED PERSON.   
 

Next up, there’s Excitement or seeking to alleviate Boredom.  
   
Reflect on these statements, do they sound familiar?  
   
*“I have a couple hours every evening on my hands and have nothing to do so, I’m bored so I watch porn. Porn gives me a quick boost and something to pass the time before I go to bed.”*  
   
*Or*  
   
*“I spend most of my time on the computer anyway for work. Porn is a nice distraction from the mundane routine from what I am doing, it’s an exciting escape into another world.”*  
   
Let’s look…. Really? Watching porn for hours, alone, is exciting? Looking at these images \- dismissing hundreds of them in search of the perfect one. Scrolling, page after page?.  

Would you look back on a Friday night of watching porn and masturbating for hours as an “exciting” Friday night? I wouldn't either. In fact, I don’t know if there is a more boring activity we could even think of.    
   
And actually, while pornography may initially seem exciting because of its taboo nature, this excitement diminishes VERY quickly as the brain becomes desensitized to these images.    
   
We then have to go seek out more extreme material to recapture the initial excitement, often to no avail.  
   
This whole process in its essence distorts our expectations of sex and intimacy, leading to even more diminished excitement overall.  
   
Guys the irony here is the same \- in pursuit of excitement through pornography, we undermine the excitement and pleasure found in authentic sexual experiences.  
   
By prioritizing the false excitement of porn, we inadvertently set ourselves up for continued disappointment and disillusionment in our real-life relationships.  
   
The nail in the coffin is you become a more and more BORING PERSON, that can never FEEL EXCITED the more porn you consume.    
   
Now let’s talk about Sexual Satisfaction  
   
   
*“I love the variety porn offers, I find everything that I want and fantasize about right at my fingertips.”*  
   
*Or*  
   
*“I could never meet these kinds of women or have these experiences in real life. Porn gives me direct and easy access to those experiences; I feel like I am living them.”*  
   
The idea that porn provides sexual satisfaction is another belief that quickly unravels when we examine it closer.  
   
While porn and masturbation together may offer temporary sexual gratification through orgasm, it often does so at the expense of true connection and emotional intimacy.  
   
We all know and many of you have experienced that over time, habitual consumption leads to sexual dysfunction and unrealistic expectations.  
   
it creates barriers to experiencing genuine sexual satisfaction with a real partner.  
   
The tragedy here is that, while we turn to pornography seeking sexual satisfaction, we inadvertently create a cycle of dissatisfaction and isolation that drives us further away from the very satisfaction we long for.  
   
This can result in feelings of inadequacy, self-doubt, and disappointment, which can further erode our overall sexual satisfaction.  
   
You become UNSATISFIABLE.   
   
Stressed out, boring, and sexually unsatisfiable.  This is the porn addict.    
   
So, what is actually happening here?   
   
Take a deep breath, when we look at it deeper we usually find that underlying feelings of stress or discomfort trigger the idea that you should go and find some type of relief.  

Your internal perpetual brainwashing tells you that the place you will find this relief is PornHub.  
   
Now the thought of PornHub starts raising your dopamine levels making you motivated to go get to your computer as fast as you can. 

You scroll endlessly, while your dopamine is surging from viewing all the images you’ve habituated yourself to.   
   
You’re flooded with all the happy brain chemicals from orgasming \- and then you CRASH.

The second it's over. You snap out of the spell.  
   
You recoil at what you’ve just done. You are NOT relaxed, NOT satisfied, and you’re back to being “bored”.  
   
And with each cycle like this my friend, it makes the feelings of stress, boredom, and dissatisfaction even worse. 

It’s like drinking salt water to quench your thirst \- the more you drink, the more dehydrated you become.   
   
Again, please…. Do not get me wrong.  
   
I am not anti-pornography.  
and  
I am not anti-masturbation.  
   
My goal is not to tear down an industry or equate porn with something evil or inherently wrong.  
   
My goal is that you are HONEST with yourself about WHY you do it, so that you may have a broader set of choices to make.  
   
And when you can wrap your mind around this endless cycle, you will come to terms with the misbelief that porn satiates you.  
   
Once you do, you’ll comprehend that you don’t consume porn because you get any real benefit from it. It’s just a lie.  
   
Society and culture pushed sexual images and ideas on you your whole life, leading you to porn. 

Innocent porn use, turned into habitual addiction by hijacking your natural brain chemistry leading you to this cycle

 \- And the only reason you don’t hop off of the addiction wheel is that you keep telling yourself that you're benefiting from it.   
   
But as you can now see you are NOT benefiting from it. 

In fact, you can walk away from porn right now, today, and you wouldn’t physically feel a thing.  
   
There is absolutely NO bodily dependency on porn.   
Quitting porn is painless when compared to chemical addictions.  
   
Quitting heroin causes actual, physical illness when going through withdrawal. 

What symptoms would you feel if you walked away today? Some agitation? Some irritability?  
   
That's it \- it's just in the mind, nothing more. It’s a mirage. It’s not difficult to stop, it's not even painful to stop, you lose nothing by stopping.  
   
You are just still TELLING YOURSELF that it will hurt. Break this belief, and you will be free. Easily.   
   
Porn does not have a hold on you.  You have a hold on IT.  You have the power to painlessly let go whenever you choose.   
   
You are doing great, keep it up\!  
Review your notes and I’ll join you in our next lesson.  
---

 

## **3.5 Our Recap** 

   
Congratulations \- and nicely done on finishing another Module\!  
Let’s go through our quick recap on what we’ve learned.  
   
SO,  
We live in a world saturated with sexual imagery and suggestions. 

These factors, when combined with our curiosity and natural urges, lead us to pornography. Pure and simple.  
   
And although porn doesn't offer real sex or physical intimacy, it still remains highly appealing to us due to its addictive nature.  
   
Comparing the addiction to pornography with other drug addictions, like cocaine for instance helps us follow the physiological pathway. We get to know our bodies better and more intimately.  
   
Both substances trigger the release of dopamine, a neurotransmitter that plays a key role in our brain's reward and pleasure systems. 

When you consume porn or cocaine, dopamine levels surge, causing those pleasurable sensations you experience.  
   
However there is a price to pay, afterward, dopamine levels drop significantly, leading to cravings for more, and more, and more.  
   
Pornography and cocaine both exploit the brain's dopamine-based pleasure/reward system, leading to the development of addiction. 

This addiction is rooted in the same neurochemical processes that govern drug addiction,  
   
You can now clearly see that the appeal of porn is fundamentally driven by chemical processes in the brain.  
   
So…What are the consequences of the dopaminergic response in the brain, which leads to this escalation and desensitization in porn consumption?  
   
The simple explanation is that the brain adapts to the stimuli, requiring more intense content to achieve the same pleasurable response, similar again to drug addiction.  
   
As we seek more intense stimuli, our ability to be aroused in general diminishes, leading to desensitization. 

This explains why you experience the need to keep going back to more and more shocking content that honestly would normally repulse or disgust you.  
   
We admitted to ourselves, the loss of intimacy is another consequence of excessive porn use. As arousal and satisfaction from porn increase, your ability to be aroused by and feel connected to your partner declines. . Insert image here (lonely man)  
   
Sex, intimacy, and arousal are interconnected aspects of human relationships, but overconsumption of pornography can disrupt this balance, leading to a decline in overall relationship satisfaction.  
   
Think about your relationships, the connection between your intimate life and your porn use. 

Honestly ask yourself if you were more intimate before or after you started to dive deep down the rabbit hole of your porn experience,  
   
Pornography consumption in its essence is isolating, as it lacks the emotional connection and interpersonal dynamics present during sex with a real partner.

It’s a sad and lonely act that only perpetuates loneliness.  
   
Furthermore, as you become more reliant on pornography, you may view sex as a purely physical act, devoid of emotional intimacy.  
   
Is this representative of a life filled with true pleasure?  
   
Quite the opposite, this social withdrawal and isolation reinforces the addiction and takes over your real active social life and opportunities for real pleasure with a capital P.  
   
We’re here to emphasize the importance of understanding the power of the mind and human physiology, so that you can overcome the chemical impulses and stop the behaviors that just don't serve you. 

This was the promise, the offer, the invitation when you started the course.  
   
Once you're aware of these limitations discussed in these previous lessons, you can choose to move beyond them, to healthier and more fulfilling forms of sexual expression and intimacy, and ultimately to live a more authentic, empowered, and connected life.  
   
So again… what the hell is so great about porn then?  
This is a great question, and one you are probably starting to ask yourself.  
   
In the lesson, "What was so great about porn?", we discussed the perceived benefits of masturbating to pornography, separating it from masturbation alone.  
   
We examined the context of watching porn, noting that it is essentially having sex alone while watching others engage in real sex. 

You may laugh and chuckle but this important realization led us to question whether it was EVER truly satisfying or even desirable.  
   
Together we acknowledged the feelings of disgust, shame, and self-loathing that often follow the act of watching porn and explored the reasons behind engaging in this behavior in the first place.  
   
We found that we often turned to porn when feeling low, stressed, or anxious, mistakenly believing it would alleviate emotional discomfort.  
   
However, the irony was that excessive porn consumption often exacerbates emotional discomfort. 

We identified three misconceptions about the benefits of porn: relaxation, excitement and alleviating boredom, and sexual satisfaction.  
   
For relaxation, we revealed the truth that porn consumption often leads to increased stress and discomfort, as the temporary dopamine-induced relief becomes more difficult to achieve due to desensitization, driving us to seek more extreme content.  
   
Honestly its quite the opposite of being truly relaxed. Ask yourself again here, when I last experienced true relaxation, was it after a porn binging session. Most likely the answer is no.  
   
Regarding excitement and alleviating boredom, we discussed how the initial novelty and thrill of porn fades quickly as the brain becomes desensitized. 

The pursuit of excitement through pornography distorts your expectations of sex and intimacy, ultimately undermining your excitement and pleasure found in authentic sexual experiences.  
   
For the misconception that porn provides sexual satisfaction, we noted that, while porn and masturbation may offer temporary gratification, they often do so at the cost of true connection and emotional intimacy.   
   
In fact, habitual consumption leads you to sexual dysfunction, unrealistic expectations, and barriers to genuine sexual satisfaction with your partner.  
   
The tragic irony is that seeking satisfaction through porn inadvertently creates a cycle of dissatisfaction and isolation, driving you FURTHER away from the satisfaction you TRULY desire. 

This can intensify your feelings of inadequacy, self-doubt, and disappointment, making you an insatiable consumer far out of touch with reality.  
    
And we discussed how the cycle of seeking relief through pornography, creates the triggers in the brain to associate relief with sites and pages you frequent. 

Dopamine levels surge as you scroll through these sites, looking for the right images, and you become flooded with feel-good chemicals when you orgasm.   
   
However, remember you quickly crash after the experience, feeling unhappy, unsatisfied, and bored.  
   
With each cycle, stress, boredom, and dissatisfaction worsen, akin to drinking saltwater to quench your thirst, only to become more dehydrated.  
   
And please remember I’m not anti-pornography or anti-masturbation and I do not aim to tear down the industry or equate porn with evil or inherent wrongness.   
   
Instead, the goal is to encourage honesty about why you engage in porn consumption, allowing for YOU the possibility of a broader set of choices.  
   
Society and culture have brainwashed you into valuing porn, while neurochemistry perpetuates the habit.  
   
If you are completely honest with yourself, you can see how there’s no real benefit to consuming porn. You have just been caught in a cycle that started with society's influence, leading to addiction through the hijacking of natural brain chemistry.  
   
The main reason you don't break free from this cycle is the belief that you benefit from it in some way as we explored earlier.  
   
And now. Rejoice, and take a deep breath, knowing that quitting porn is painless compared to other addictions.  
   
Walking away from porn may cause some temporary agitation or irritability, but it is a mental challenge rather than a physical one.  
   
By breaking this belief in the benefits of porn, you will quickly see you can free yourself easily.  
   
Really, you have made it this far and I honor you for that\!  
You’ve absorbed an amazing amount of material in this module, and you should be very proud of yourself.  
   
Up next, you’ll find a guided meditation and in your resource for this module are your journal prompts to help you process what you’ve just learned.  
   
Listen to the meditation this evening before bed and take your time with the prompts. Give it everything you have, you deserve it.  
   
So, once you feel ready, I will meet you in our next video where I’ll walk you through the guided meditation.

See you soon.  
---

**3.6 A Guided Meditation**   
   
Welcome to this guided meditation for empowerment, self-compassion, and relaxation. This meditation is designed to help you overcome porn addiction and find inner strength. Please find a comfortable position, either sitting or lying down, where you can relax and remain undisturbed for the next 15 minutes.  
   
Let's begin by setting an intention for this session. Take a moment to consider what you hope to achieve, whether it's self-compassion, inner strength, or a sense of peace. Hold that intention in your mind as we move through this meditation.  
   
Now, gently close your eyes and bring your attention to your breath.  
   
Take a slow, deep breath in through your nose, and then exhale slowly through your mouth.  
   
Allow your breath to guide you into a state of relaxation.  
   
Continue to breathe deeply and slowly, focusing on the sensation of the air entering and leaving your body.  
   
As you breathe, let's begin with progressive muscle relaxation.  
   
Start by focusing on your feet. Tense the muscles in your feet as tightly as you can, holding for a few seconds, and then release, allowing the tension to melt away.   
   
Move up to your calves and repeat the process. Tense, hold, and release.  
   
Continue the process – moving up through your thighs,  
   
Buttocks,  
   
Abdomen,  
   
lower back,  
   
upper back,  
   
Shoulders,  
   
Arms,  
   
Hands,  
   
Neck,  
   
And finally your face.  
   
Take your time and remember to breathe deeply and slowly as you tense and release each muscle group.  
   
Now that your body is relaxed, let's move on to the visualization.  
   
Imagine yourself in a serene and peaceful place, a place that brings you comfort and joy.  
It could be a beach, a forest, or a mountain landscape.  
   
Take a moment to picture this place in your mind and try to engage all your senses.   
   
Hear the sounds around you, whether it's the ocean waves, rustling leaves, or a gentle breeze.  
   
Smell the fresh air and feel the warmth of the sun or the coolness of the shade on your skin.  
   
As you immerse yourself in this peaceful place, allow yourself to feel completely safe and at ease. This is a space where you can let go of any guilt, shame, or self-judgment.  
   
Throughout this meditation, I will introduce some positive affirmations. As you hear each affirmation, repeat it silently to yourself, and let the words resonate deep within you.  
   
"I am strong and capable."  
   
"I am deserving of love and respect."  
"I am in control of my actions and choices."  
   
Feel the truth and power of these affirmations as you continue to breathe deeply and relax in your peaceful place.  
   
Allow these words to help you build self-esteem and self-compassion.  
   
Now, gently bring your awareness back to your breath, and take a few more deep, slow breaths.  
   
With each breath, feel a sense of empowerment and inner strength growing within you. Acknowledge your commitment to overcoming porn addiction and the positive changes you are making in your life.  
   
As this meditation comes to an end, take a moment to express gratitude to yourself for dedicating this time to your well-being and personal growth.  
   
Remember that you are worthy of love, respect, and inner peace.  
   
Now, Slowly begin to bring your awareness back to your surroundings, listening to the sounds around you and feeling the surface beneath you.  
   
Gently wiggle your fingers and toes to reconnect with your body.  
   
When you feel ready, take one deep, cleansing breath, and then slowly open your eyes.  
   
As you return to your surroundings, carry with you the sense of empowerment, self-compassion, and relaxation that you cultivated during this meditation.   
   
Remember that you have the strength and resilience to overcome any challenges that come your way.  
   
Thank you for joining me in this guided meditation.  
   
May you continue to find inner strength and self-compassion on your journey to overcoming porn addiction.  
   
Peace and Blessings, my friend \-

## **MODULE 4: ADDICTION IS GOOD**

## **Outline**

## 4.1 Reframing Addiction 

## 4.2 Depletive Vs. Accretive Inputs 

## 4.3 Use Addiction to Fuel the Life Of Your Dreams

## 4.4 Our Recap

## 4.5 A Guided Meditation

##  

## **MODULE 4: ADDICTION IS GOOD**

##  

## **4.1 Reframing Addiction** 

##  

Welcome back\!  
So, in our previous module, I wanted you to completely and genuinely understand two things:  
   
The first, is that you are physiologically addicted to Porn \- there’s an actual neuro-chemical mechanism that exists in the brain, which Porn has hijacked and it’s spinning like a hamster wheel.  
   
And as for the second – the only reason you can’t seem to get off this hamster wheel, is because you’ve been brainwashed so badly, that you now lie to yourself \- convinced that you enjoy this hamster wheel, when in fact you are miserable and exhausted\!  
   
We’ll talk about my second point in another module, but now I want to go deeper into the first point \- You are an addict.  
   
Deep breath,  
Let’s spin that statement and look at it from another vantage point \-    
   
For most of us, The word “addict” or “addiction” conjures up all kinds of images, ideas, and preconceived notions in our heads.  
   
In my work in addiction & recovery and to a large degree in mental health, I’ve encountered many men and women who delayed their healing because they were afraid and embarrassed to be labeled as someone with a problem.  
   
They often expressed regret over precious time lost hiding their addictions. You see, shame and embarrassment are powerful experiences we fight hard to avoid, even harder at times than giving up the addition, it was heartbreaking to hear their stories.  
   
Yet they are correct in a sense, and I could understand the behavior completely.  
   
When we think of addiction, we think of strung-out heroin addicts, homeless alcoholics, and people stealing from their family members to get their fix.  
   
“Addiction” is heavily stigmatized and mainly thought of as pathology \- something has broken that needs to be fixed.  
   
And this can be true, those of us who have encountered what we recognize as addiction are horrified by the havoc that addiction wreaks in people's lives.  
   
Addiction can be absolutely devastating \- it can be one of the saddest and most unsettling things to witness across all human experience.   
   
But we’re going to look at addiction differently, remember?  
And what I’d like to propose is that “Addiction” is neither inherently bad nor inherently good \- it is simply a Neurological Mechanism.  
   
So while we recognize addiction in some of the most horrifying displays of human self-destruction, what we have usually not been trained to see, is that “Addiction” is also the driver of some of the most beautiful and heroic displays of human achievement, actualization and manifestation.  
   
If you think about it in this new light, Addiction can be a stunningly beautiful thing. However, we’re not taught this.  
   
Here \- Let me explain a bit further:  
   
The human brain has developed through millions of years of evolution. The brain's job is to serve you, protect you, and get you what you want.  
   
And Central to the job of getting you what you want is the dopaminergic system. It exists to motivate you and compel you forward in your endeavors. To move into action.  
   
When you are excited to go do something \- that’s dopamine.  
When you are giddy with anticipation of something you want \- that’s dopamine.   
   
Dopamine surging through your brain is the reason you get up in the morning and go do ANYTHING.  
   
On the flip side, when you have low dopamine levels, you’re lethargic and sad \- you feel hopeless and lack the energy to simply pour yourself a cup of coffee or take a shower.  
   
Dopamine is fundamental to our existence, and I would argue, maybe the most important and most definitely a powerful mechanism for determining the direction of one’s life.   
   
So I’d like to think of the dopaminergic system as a “Compounding Machine”.   
   
So what does this machine do?  
Well, the compounding machine literally takes a thought, motivates you to take action, that thought then becomes reality, and then makes sure you *want it again.*  
   
The fact that you have a literal mechanism in your brain that turns thoughts into reality is miraculous \- but the real power is in the last step of the cycle \- making sure you WANT IT AGAIN.  
   
The word “Again”…………is the fundamental ingredient of creation and manifestation.  
You see repetition is at the heart of every core biological process in nature and the universe.  
   
In nature, iterative processes like cell division, genetic replication, and the formation of complex structures like crystals and snowflakes rely on repetition.  
   
The process of evolution itself is based on the repetition of genetic information and the gradual accumulation of adaptations over time.  
   
And we’ve all been taught the power of repetition in our lives:  
   
Let me simplify it. Not because you might not understand it but because it’s helpful to have a picture in your mind of what this means.  
   
Want to become a great poet? \- write thousands of poems.  
Want to become a world class basketball player? Play basketball every single day \- for a decade.  
   
Repetition is the process of creation.  
And that process needs an engine.  
That engine is your “compounding machine” \- take a thought, insert it into the machine, turn it into reality, then repeat that action over and over, and watch that mother fucker grow.  
   
Kobe, Jordan, Lebron, Picasso, Monet, Dali, Jobs, Musk. Think about it, these are elites who have honed their crafts to the point of obsession. You can think of your own personal hero gender doesn’t matter. They use the same compounding machine to become their greatest version of themselves.   
   
You follow what I'm saying?  
   
Simply Put \- The mind is THAT powerful \- meaning what you put into it, you will manifest \- perhaps even 10-fold.  
   
The catch here is because of this immense power \- your mind can be your savior, or it can lead to your destruction.   
   
But make no mistake \- the wiring of the brain to put you into a cycle of intense repetition is 100% natural \- and even if you’re an “addict” of porn, smoking, drugs, or any other vice \- your brain is operating exactly the way it's meant to.  
   
Your brain isn’t broken \- you’re just using your “Compounding Machine” incorrectly.  
   
Using it the right way ultimately comes down to what you put into it \- You’re either adding “Depleting Inputs” or “Accretive Inputs”.  
   
I’ll say it again. Your brain isn’t broken \- you’re just using your “Compounding Machine” incorrectly.  
   
And using it the right way ultimately comes down to what you put into it \- You’re either adding “Depleting Inputs” or “Accretive Inputs”.  
   
Phew I can feel the juices moving. I hope you can too. If not jump up and down, put on your favorite song and dance till you can feel your heart beating in your chest.  
   
This is excellent work you are doing\!  
   
Get your dance on and I’ll see you in our next lesson.  
We’ll discuss the difference between the two.  
   
See you in a few\!  
---

 

## **4.2 Depletive vs. Accretive Inputs** 

   
Heyoo,  
Welcome back. Let’s get right into the differences between Depletive vs. Accretive Inputs, and the impact they have on our lives.  
   
Also, what happens when we feed these inputs into our dopaminergic system \- or our “Compounding Machine”.   
   
Simply put, a “Depletive Input” is a thought, action, or behavior, when repeated over and over will have negative outcomes, by depleting \- or draining from the quality of one’s life.  
   
An “Accretive Input” is the opposite \- a thought, action or behavior when repeated over and over will have a positive outcome and be an additive to the quality of one’s life.  
   
Seems somewhat straightforward. Right?  
But there are a couple of key things to understand about these types of inputs to make this concept usable.   
   
Alright, so let's take a look at what some depleting inputs look like and what they can do in our lives when repeated over and over.  
   
Take something simple like sugar.  
Sugar tastes soooo good.  
Yes? Oooh man \- YES\!  
   
So much so, that even the mere thought of something sweet actually triggers a rise in our dopamine, motivating us to go devour that cupcake.  
   
But what happens if we eat cupcakes every day?  
We gain weight right?  
   
Our blood sugar rises, we have energy crashes, and we can develop long-term health issues. The repetition of one simple, seemingly harmless thing \- compounded that one cupcake into a lifetime of suffering at the hands of sugar.  
   
Sugar is depleting because the more of it you consume, the more it takes away from the quality of your life. And this… is sadly the condition of millions of people in this country who are addicted to sugar.   
   
I’m sure you have put two and two together at this point and can see how porn is a depletive input in your life.
