We drop that one video into our compounding machine, and boom \- it has now multiplied into thousands of videos, hundreds of hours, second and third-order problems in our relationships, our sex lives, our self-image, and our view of the outside world.  
   
Porn is “depleting” because the more and more you do it, the more and more it takes away from the quality of your life.   
   
Note that these inputs are *small.* It didn’t take you eating 30 cupcakes to trigger the compounding machine.  
   
It just took one, maybe a couple.  
   
The machine did the rest.  
 And It WILL ensure that you “crave” another one shortly. 

In fact, it's so good at what it does, it will even time your craving to the exact hour every single day – the machine is meticulous about its ONLY job \- to compound the input.   
   
On the contrary \- Exercising, meditation, and saving money are all Accretive inputs. These are all inputs that if you repeat them over and over, will lead to an increase in the quality of your life.  
   
Exercise daily, and you’ll become stronger and healthier, leading to many other benefits \- such as becoming more attractive, being in a better mood more often, and higher self-confidence.  
   
Meditation, if repeated methodically, leads to clarity of mind, lower stress, and personal insight, which lead to more enjoyable life experiences.  
   
Saving money, when done habitually, will literally compound your financial position.  
   
We all know people who do these things religiously. What we don’t necessarily recognize is that they are “addicted” to these things, and that's why they do them over and over again.  
   
Their brain responds by providing dopamine when these behaviors are thought of or engaged in, and then it drops the dopamine, so they crave doing it again.  
   
The mechanism is the same.  
The input is different.  
And therefore, the output is different.   
   
We are incredibly fortunate that we have been given this gift.  
   
HOWEVER, there is one VERY important distinction I want to make between typical depleting inputs and typical accretive inputs.  
   
Depleting inputs are usually CHEAP sources of dopamine.  
And Accretive inputs are usually EXPENSIVE sources of dopamine.  
   
What do I mean by that?  
   
Well, a cheap source of dopamine will typically require very little energy to get, will spike dopamine immediately, will spike it relatively high, and then will drop back below baseline relatively quickly.  
   
Porn is the perfect example \- you consume it with little effort, (push of a button) your dopamine spikes quickly, giving you that rush, and then pretty quickly afterward you’ll be back to feeling like shit \- and soon craving more porn.  
   
As opposed to say, exercise which requires that you exert energy. So It raises your dopamine more gradually, and not as high, BUT the dopamine remains elevated for a longer period of time \- which is why you’re in a good mood well past the time you left the gym  
   
So the message here is this……What you want to do is become addicted to accretive inputs, and once you become hooked, they will compound on themselves and create the life of your dreams \- which YOU will be in control of.   
   
It’s amazing and inspiring, isn’t it?\!  
Reflect on it , take a break, let it sink in, and I’ll catch you in the following lesson.

## ---

##  

## **4.3 Use Addiction to Fuel the Life Of Your Dreams**

##  

Heyoo, welcome back\!  
   
That last lesson was a big one, if it helps go back and watch it again as many times as you need so it sinks in deep. 

Deep to the point, you feel the power you possess through your brilliantly designed machine that you are in charge of.  
   
Now, how can we use addiction to fuel the life of our dreams?  
   
Simply put, the key to creating the life of your dreams is to feed your Compounding Machine Accretive Inputs instead of Depleting ones.  
   
Now, you may be thinking to yourself, “well that's easy to SAY, but much harder to do\!”. After all, we all know that exercise is good for us \- but we still don't do it. We know not to order that fast food, or eat that last pint of ice cream but we still go through it and feel the pain of it later.  
   
Clearly “Knowing” hasn’t been enough.  
   
There has to be something else that pushes us into action. And in this lesson, we’ll talk about what you need to understand, for you to start feeding your addictive mind accretive inputs.   
   
So \- Why do we tend to go after depleting inputs, instead of accretive inputs when we KNOW they will lead to better outcomes for us?  
   
The answer is it has to do with our relationship with pain and discomfort.  
   
Here’s the typical cycle:  
   
We try all kinds of inputs early in life, mainly through curiosity. Smoking, drugs, porn, exercise, meditation, and the like. A mixture of both Depletive and Accretive inputs.   
   
And, we learn from those experiences which have immediate dopamine effects , usually the cheap dopamine inputs, and which have long term effects.   
   
However, we’re not necessarily strongly drawn to doing it again, UNTIL…  
We face some sort of difficulty, stress, or pain.  
   
Then our brain remembers what can “medicate” or “dull” the pain or discomfort, and it signals a craving for that input.  
   
We go obtain that input, temporarily avoiding that discomfort.  
   
The brain now associates that input with “medication”, and when friction in life occurs, the mind thinks of the “medication”, then the dopamine spikes, and you enter the cycle.   
   
And this all happens subconsciously.

On the surface, you're not thinking while smoking cigarettes, watching porn, or snorting cocaine you are self-medicating or avoiding pain.  
 \- No \- you THINK you're doing it because you ACTUALLY like it.  
   
And in that moment, you’ve now entered into a cycle of addiction to a depleting input \- which will usually wreak havoc on your life.  
   
Many clients that come to me are in a phase where they have reached a point of collapse, the way they are living, their behaviors, and coping mechanisms do not bring them any relief from the stress and anxiety they are experiencing.  
   
We work on how to avoid slipping into that cycle.  
And we do that, by becoming resilient\!  
   
But how?  
   
Well, when life brings us difficulties: stress, anxiety, pain, disappointment, sadness \- we tend to medicate that pain with pleasure.  
   
The key to becoming the type of person who gets addicted to accretive inputs is to begin to raise one's tolerance for stress and pain 

\- by engaging in things that are painful but profitable.  
   
This seems counterintuitive and probably doesn’t sound very fun, but the paradox of pain and pleasure is that both tend to lead to the other.  
   
If we go back and take a look at the list of our “accretive input” addicted heroes. It’s easy to see each one of them suffered for their craft.  
   
It took an immense amount of tolerance to go day and day out into the gym, the office, the studio, the arena, to face the physical and mental stress and go through it.  
   
The result of building this tolerance is everything we long for, the championships, financial success, mind blowing masterpieces, the experiences of pleasure with a capital P.  
   
We don’t even need to think that grandiose. Even our relationships to others require us to build tolerance, to stay authentic, move through the difficulties, to build resilience, to stay grounded in the pain that invariably comes with being in a relationship.  
   
This means being misunderstood in our needs, falling out, getting sick, needing help, coming together. If we only go for the good times, the vacations, the honeymoon phases, then when the shit really hits the fan, we don’t have the emotional backbone to go on and we collapse.   
   
My point is,  
The more pleasure we obtain, the harder it is to feel true pleasure, and the easier it is to feel pain \- we become fragile or “soft”.  
   
But on the flip-side, when we purposefully pursue pain \- exercise, studying, sacrificing for others or higher purposes, challenging ourselves to be authentic \- we strengthen ourselves and feel less pain throughout life,  
   
Life becomes easier to endure and small irritants don’t send us into worry and panic, cause us to close the door and disappear into porn \- we become more solid at our core, letting the stress move through us.   
   
This is why it is not uncommon to see people who seemingly have it all, can also be so miserable.  
   
Their life is full of nothing but pleasure \- there isn’t enough challenge, friction, adversity, or pain \- and so the pleasures of life become dull.  
   
The 5-star meal no longer impresses, the 50th hot stone massage, is just “meh”. Too much pleasure always leads to pain.   
   
So, what we want to do is all in the next step we take,

When we feel discomfort, stress, anxiety, or feelings we don't like, instead of going for the depleting input that will temporarily numb that pain \- we need to allow ourselves to FEEL that anxiety.  
   
In our daily lives we need to lean more into pain \- do things that require MORE from us \- not less.  
   
Go to the gym, meditate, go for a run, call someone and talk about your feelings, cook a really healthy meal, journal.  
   
And you may find that a few of these things will have SOME level of “pain” associated with doing them, but paradoxically, the process of doing them makes you less prone to pain\!  
   
When repeated over and over, you will build up a tolerance for stress, anxiety, and pain \- what used to knock you down, shake you off your axis, now you just walk straight through.  
   
The Depletive inputs you would have craved will seem less appealing now because you know you've been brainwashed into thinking you like those things.  
   
Pain is your partner \- and pleasure should only be the reward you get for faithfully engaging with the type of pain that continuously builds you up.   
   
If you adopt this type of mentality \- you will begin to start replacing the depleting inputs you feed your mind, with accretive inputs, and the virtuous cycle of compounding greatness will come into your life.  
   
Become addicted to getting pleasure *as a reward for pain.*   
Become your own hero\!\!  
   
Alright, amazing work \- review your notes, and I will see you in our next lesson\!

##  

## ---

## **4.4 Our Recap**

##  

heyooo, \- nice work so far \- are you ready for a quick review?  
   
\- let’s get to it\!  
   
So, in this module, we have explored the concept of addiction and challenged the preconceived notions surrounding it. This might be a new concept to you, which is OK.  
   
You don’t have to agree, just continue to listen with an open mind.  
   
In this new framework, instead of viewing addiction as inherently bad or good, consider addiction simply as a neurological mechanism driven by the brain's dopaminergic system.  
   
This system, we called the "Compounding Machine," is responsible for motivation and the desire to repeat actions. It plays a critical role in creation and manifestation through repetition, which is at the core of many of our natural processes.  
   
Through this, we came to understand that the brain is not broken in cases of addiction, but rather, it is being used in the wrong way.  
   
The key to using this mechanism effectively lies in what gets fed into the "Compounding Machine," which can either be "Depleting Inputs" or "Accretive Inputs."  
   
Depletive Inputs we defined as thoughts, actions, or behaviors that lead to negative outcomes when repeated, while Accretive Inputs resulted in positive outcomes when repeated.  
   
We reviewed a few Examples of Depletive Inputs, such as sugar and porn, which illustrate how these inputs can have detrimental effects on our life when compounded.  
   
Accretive Inputs, on the other hand, like exercise, meditation, and saving money, improve the quality of life when repeated.  
   
You’ve probably already experienced this in your life in some way but perhaps were not aware of it.  
   
Accretive Inputs often require more effort and provide a more gradual and longer-lasting dopamine response than the quick, cheap dopamine spikes from Depletive Inputs.  
   
That said, the goal then, is to become addicted to Accretive Inputs, which will lead you to a fulfilling and self-directed life.  
   
Accepting this notion of using addiction to fuel your life’s dreams by feeding the "Compounding Machine" with accretive inputs rather than depleting ones might come as a relief to some of you.  
   
Perhaps you know it intuitively and have already started the process of replacing harmful habits but are still struggling with getting things moving along on the right track.  
   
You might also still be wondering what to do about the stress, anxiety, pain and discomfort that you feel, and are looking for some relief from these pain points.  
   
You were also introduced to the typical cycle of addiction and its connection to pain and discomfort. 

We highlighted the importance of understanding and changing one's mindset during times of stress and pain, as that is when harmful habits are often triggered. Feeling stress and anxiety? Look for a quick way out.  
   
We also dove into the paradox of pain and pleasure, demonstrating that purposefully pursuing pain through activities such as exercise, studying, or sacrificing for higher purposes can lead to a more fulfilling life.  
   
By embracing pain, you’ll become more resilient and capable of finding pleasure in the simplest things.  
   
When you do experience moments of discomfort and of course they will come because this is part of life., it is crucial to lean into pain by engaging in activities that require more effort.  
   
This approach builds tolerance to stress, anxiety, and pain, allowing you to walk through challenges with ease. 

Let’s change our relationship with pain from an adversary to a partner, with pleasure being the reward for faithfully engaging with pain.   
   
This module offered you the opportunity to adopt a new mentality that replaces depleting inputs with accretive ones.  
   
By becoming addicted to deriving pleasure as a reward for embracing pain, you can start a virtuous cycle of compounding greatness in your life, ultimately taking control of your own energy, well-being and happiness.  
   
Now doesn’t that sound like a great experience?  
Yes, it does my friend, yes it does.  
   
Now, before moving on to our next module \- work through the Journal prompts you’ll find in the resources for this module, and clear yourself to enter into a state of reflection for our guided meditation.  
   
I’ll see you there\!  
   
---

4.5 A Guided Meditation  
   
Welcome to this guided meditation, designed to help you connect with your inner courage and overcome the obstacles you face in your journey.  
   
Please find a quiet and comfortable space where you can sit or lie down without any distractions. Close your eyes and take a few deep breaths, inhaling slowly through your nose and exhaling gently through your mouth.  
   
As you breathe in, imagine you're inhaling courage, strength, and determination. As you exhale, release any doubts, fears, and negative thoughts. Allow your body to relax and your mind to become more focused.  
   
Now, visualize a warm and comforting light surrounding your entire body.  
   
This light represents your inner courage, the power that exists within you to overcome your addiction. As you continue to breathe deeply, feel this light growing stronger and more vibrant.  
   
Take a moment to consider the obstacles you face in overcoming your addiction.   
   
Acknowledge them without judgment and recognize that they are simply challenges to be faced and conquered. With each breath, imagine yourself drawing in the strength and resilience you need to face these obstacles head-on.  
   
As you inhale, imagine the warm light within you growing brighter and more powerful.  
   
This light is the embodiment of your courage, determination, and inner strength. With each exhale, feel your fears and doubts being released, making room for the courage within you to grow.  
   
Now, picture yourself standing at the foot of a mountain, representing your journey to this point. The path ahead may be steep and challenging, but you are filled with the courage and determination needed to make that climb.  
   
Feel the power within you, ready to propel you forward.  
   
Take a moment to visualize yourself climbing this mountain step by step. With each step, your courage grows stronger, and your determination becomes more resolute. As you climb higher, the obstacles you once faced now seem smaller and more manageable.  
   
As you near the top of the mountain, you pause to take in the view. You can see how far you've come and how much you've accomplished.  
   
You feel a deep sense of pride and gratitude for your inner strength and resilience. As you stand at the summit, you realize that you have the power to overcome any obstacle on your path to recovery.  
   
Take a few more deep breaths, allowing the feeling of courage and accomplishment to wash over you. With each breath, feel your connection to this inner strength grow stronger and more secure.  
   
Now, slowly begin to bring your awareness back to the present moment.  
   
Feel your body resting on the surface beneath you, and become aware of the sounds around you.  
   
Take a deep breath in and gently wiggle your fingers and toes.  
And when you feel ready, open your eyes, returning to the present with a renewed sense of courage and determination.  
   
Remember, you have the power within you to overcome any obstacle on your path to freedom. Carry this courage with you as you continue your journey, and know that you are never alone.

**MODULE 5: IT'S ALL IN YOUR HEAD**  
**Outline**  
5.1 Brainwashed  
5.2 Addiction Is A Tool  
5.3 Quit Porn By Just Walking Away  
5.4 MEDITATION & Journal Prompts  
 

**MODULE 5: IT’S ALL IN YOUR HEAD**

## **5.1 Brainwashed**

   
Heyoo\! Welcome back  
Let's now take a moment to digest all of the concepts we’ve learned in the past 4 Modules.  
   
What will be evident to you when looking at it all together – is that it's literally ALL IN YOUR HEAD.  
   
Your porn addiction does not have a vice grip on you. YOU have a vice grip on IT \- and all you need to do is let go.  
   
By this point, you should be seeing the ridiculousness of this addiction, but let's walk back through, and really hammer it home \- this is ABSOLUTELY not as hard as you’ve made it out to be, and you’re one step from walking away.  
   
You are brainwashed.  
Society sells you sex,  
Every. Single. Day. of your life.  
   
Sex is a fundamental driver of human instinct, and the hyper capitalistic world we live in has tapped straight into that in order to turn a profit.  
   
On top of that, technological innovation, also driven by market competition and profit, developed the most ingenious, and insidious way to inject “sex concentrate” directly from your computer into the most potent mechanism within your brain, sending you into a neruo-chemical cycle of wanting and craving.   
   
However now, YOU are the one continuing to brainwash yourself.  
   
You are in fact craving something that you actually DO NOT enjoy. You do NOT get any benefit from porn usage \- but you TELL yourself that you do.  
   
You crave it, yet you loathe it.  
You tell yourself that it relaxes you \- but it only makes you a more anxiety ridden person  
   
You tell yourself that you do it for excitement \- but the more you do it, the less excitement you feel.  
   
You tell yourself that it’s sexually satisfying \- but in reality right after you do it, you are disgusted with yourself.  
   
That's sexual satisfaction?  
Of course not.  
   
Having sex alone is not heroic.  
Having sex alone, while viewing others having sex is humiliating \- if it wasn’t we wouldn’t be so secretive about it.  
   
You’re not proud of yourself for watching porn.  
There’s no trophies being handed out for who can jack off to the most porn.  
   
Jacking off to porn is basically a completely fruitless endeavor, that at minimum brings you zero value, but most likely is depleting from the quality of your life in serious ways.  
   
And let me be clear \- There is absolutely nothing that you would lose by quitting porn.   
   
On top of the nonsense you’re telling yourself why you need porn and why you like it so much, you are also conning yourself by telling yourself that it’s HARD to quit.  
   
Ha\! No it's not\!  
   
If you don't watch porn what will happen?  
Will your head explode?  
Will you begin vomiting uncontrollably?  
Will you fall ill?  
Will you die?  
   
NO. NO. NO. NO. NO\!  
Absolutely nothing will happen if you quit watching porn.  
   
In fact, walking away from porn is not difficult \- because it is only in your head. 

There are ZERO physical withdrawal effects from simply stopping.  
   
None.  
Zilch.  
   
The most that will happen is that you may want some porn at some point.

But honestly, if you’ve really internalized what I’ve shown you in this course, you won’t even want it again.  
   
The brain chemistry will kick in and tug a bit and give you a jones for it \- but if you actually sit and FEEL what that jones feels like, it's mild.  
It’s not a big deal.  
   
Honestly, it's all in your head.  
No boogie monster is coming to get you.  
   
You won't break down, because you don't need it.  
Your body doesn’t need it.  
Your body doesn't even want it.  
   
When you truly understand the extent to which you have been brainwashed, and that you are brainwashing yourself, it’ll be extremely easy for you to walk away from your porn addiction.   
   
Does that make sense?  
If not, you can always go back and redo the modules which you have doubts about. 

Re-read your journal entries and ask yourself if this is the life you want to continue living, or if you want to keep on moving towards self-empowerment and a life of true connection and pleasure\!  
   
See you in the next lesson.  
---

 

## **5.2 Addiction Is a Tool**

   
Heyooo –  
   
Welcome back to the light.  
Let’s keep the ball rolling, see where we’ve been and where we are going next.  
   
Remember brainwashing is what keeps you in the dark, not even really trying to search for a way out.  
   
But the “addiction” itself to porn is neuro-chemical. And the path to start finding your way out of the maze is to understand this mechanism.  
   
Something triggers you to think of porn, your brain lights up some dopamine, the dopamine drives you to go view porn, and you scroll endlessly through videos, sending your dopamine through the roof until you finally masturbate.  
   
Then the dopamine quickly subsides, eventually dropping below its baseline, triggering another craving \- and now you’re back.  
   
That dopaminergic cycle is foundational to driving human motivation, and when you drop porn into it, it’ll motivate the hell out of you to go get it.  
   
I see this clearly when clients tell me about the risky behavior associated with their porn consumption. 

Watching at work, on the train or in public places. Computers at home that they share with their children. It’s the dopamine spike that propels them.   
   
Look, porn spikes your dopamine the same way cocaine does \- it's easy to get, it spikes your dopamine fast and high and gives you that instant gratification \- which is the dopamine that you want.  
   
That said, you need to understand, this is all VERY normal and common.  
   
And the porn addiction you have, you share with millions of other people, both men, and women, in this country and worldwide\!  
   
Porn addiction is the newest, fastest-growing epidemic, yet for obvious reasons \- no one wants to talk about it. 

Fortunately, you have come to recognize something has to change and it brought you here.  
   
So don’t worry, ill remind you again, your addicted mind isn’t broken \- it's working just fine, and your porn addiction is not hard to walk away from. 

In FACT, your addictive mind is EXACTLY what you will use to transcend your negative addictions and create the life you truly want.   
   
Your brain was designed, through billions of years of evolution, to take the thoughts and desires you have and turn them into reality.  
   
And the dopaminergic mechanism that we just laid out is the primary vehicle it uses to do that.  
   
It's your Compounding Machine.  
   
Meaning \- If you are addicted to porn, it's working just fine.  
The problem isn’t your brain, the problem is what you’re feeding it.  
   
The Compound Machine does not care what you put into it.  
Whatever you put in, its job is to compound it and grow it through repetition.  
   
Put in drugs, it’ll say “cool, we're gonna do that again, and again, again, and again”. Put in porn, it’ll say “cool, we're gonna do that over and over and over”.  
   
But fortunately, you can recognize these are Depleting Inputs.

The more you compound these things, the worse your quality of life becomes.  
   
A life of abundant and fulfilling growth and happiness comes from feeding your brain with Accretive Inputs.  
   
Things that when compounded, bring you growth, joy, expansion, and actualization of your highest self. 

When Accretive Inputs are compounded, your dreams come true.  
   
These are things like exercising, reading, meditating, saving money, making money, and doing for others.  
   
If you can become addicted to these types of inputs – I guarantee your life will quickly begin to expand, as will the joy you’ll bring into your life and the lives of the people around you.  
   
Making the choice to make Accretive Inputs a routine is not difficult either but requires the understanding of why you routinely choose Depletive Inputs.  
   
You chose them because you are in pain and discomfort.   
   
I can tell you through my own personal experience, pain and discomfort are a part of life. We are living, conscious beings, living amongst all other living conscious beings, competing, and negotiating for our comfort.  
   
We even have to negotiate with the laws of physics for our comfort. Gravity itself is applying pressure on us at all times.  
   
The brain has evolved to help us find comfort, seek safety, and avoid pain.  
You see \- Early in our lives we try new experiences, including porn, drugs, alcohol, sugar, salt, adventure \- we test out every type of experience there is.  
   
We try it, and our brain remembers what it did for us. And when we’re in pain or discomfort, the brain wants to find the quickest, cheapest way to alleviate or avoid that pain or discomfort.  
   
Yet when we don’t pay close attention to our response to discomfort, we easily find ourselves addicted to Depleting inputs  
   
The key concept to grasp here is that pain and discomfort are NOT to be medicated or avoided. Instead of avoiding or medicating pain, you must become MORE TOLERANT of pain, stress, and discomfort.  
   
And the way to do this is by engaging in Accretive Inputs.  
Remember \- Accretive inputs are not cheap. They cost.  
   
They require exertion of energy.  
They require patience.  
They may even require some sacrifice.  
   
These are the things then when you become addicted to them, your tolerance for stress and discomfort just continues to rise. You become stronger. Think about the adage that which doesn’t kill me only makes me stronger\!\!  
   
Pain and stress don’t get lighter \- YOU BECOME STRONGER. And when you’re stronger, you won’t even feel the need for the Depleting Inputs.   
   
Nice work \- I’ll meet you in our next lesson.  
---

 

## **5.3 Quit Porn by Just Walking Away**

   
Heyoo- so we just learned how to become stronger through accretive inputs, well, my friend \- now it’s time to just walk away\!  
   
Quitting porn is simple once you realize how you’ve brainwashed yourself to believe that you actually enjoy it or get anything out of it.   
Once you really have grasped this, it will be as if a veil was lifted from over your eyes.  
   
Your behavior will seem ridiculous to you.  
You will view others who are still stuck in the porn cycle with pity.  
   
And even though you may have a pang or craving, it will seem small and easily ignorable.  
   
You will quit quite easily, and once you do, there will literally be NO negative consequences.  
   
You’ll just simply be free.  
   
What you WILL need to do, however, so that your Compounding Machine can create a new life for you is to manage your stress, pain, fear, and anxiety better.  
   
These are triggers for Depleting Inputs, and we want to re-train our brains to get hooked on Accretive Inputs.  
Right? \- RIGHT\!  
   
So, first, you need to NOTICE when you are feeling the type of discomfort that would normally trigger you.  
   
And you need to sit with it.  
   
Just sit still and allow yourself to feel that discomfort without attempting to medicate or avoid it \- those activities will usually be depleting inputs anyway.  
   
We want to move away from reacting to our stress in the moment and move towards managing and improving our stress tolerance through a routine of daily Accretive Inputs.  
   
Again the stress load will not lighten \- you will become stronger \- more resilient to stress.   
   
Our routine of Accretive Inputs, are the building blocks of our core.  
They are simple.  
They are repetitive.  
   
And they will compound on themselves and increase your stress tolerance by leaps and bounds. They are simple but incredibly powerful.  
   
We talked about accretive inputs in a previous module and I want to clarify something.    
I told you that to become more pain tolerant, you need to expose yourself to painful things.  
   
The point I was making was that Accretive Inputs, unlike Depleting Inputs, usually require MORE effort, energy, time, focus, etc.  
This is true, BUT, in reality \- these things are not ACTUALLY painful.  
   
This is another type of mental brainwashing that we perform on ourselves. We think of going to the gym, and we have a sense of dread. We imagine discomfort, we imagine “suffering”.  
   
But once we actually get up, get through that feeling, and get inside the gym, how do we feel?  
Is it really ever as bad as that initial dread??

Does it ACTUALLY hurt so bad that you consider yourself to be suffering?  
   
No, in reality it's quite the opposite \- once we actually drag ourselves through that mental barrier of “I don’t want to”, and we get in the gym, we feel fine exercising.   
   
And then, when we’re finished, we feel GREAT.  
We feel proud.  
We feel good about ourselves.  
We feel like we are moving forward in life.  
   
The same holds true for spending time with your friends, loved ones, going outside for fresh air, engaging in a hobby, building something with your hands, doing a selfless act for others, the list goes on and we’ll come back to this again later.  
   
But for now, I want you to think about it like this \-the “pain” associated with Accretive Inputs is not actually real \- that is also in your head. 

You just need to make it past the “i don’t want to” barrier, and you’ll be there.  
   
Accretive Inputs require more effort, but “effort” doesn’t equal “pain”.  
   
If you can move past that barrier, usually only a handful of times, you will start to become more immune to stress, less likely to use depleting inputs, and most importantly – you will become “addicted” to Accretive behaviors that will compound greatness into your life.  
   
Your time is now\!\!  
Take a couple of deep breaths \- take a break and head over to your resource section for an important journal prompt (Letter to Self). when you’re ready I will catch up with you for the meditation.  
---

 

## **5.4 MEDITATION & Journal Prompts**

Before you begin the meditation, make sure you are in a comfortable and relaxed position, either seated or lying down.  
   
Begin by taking a deep breath in through your nose, filling your lungs completely. Hold it for a moment, and then slowly exhale through your mouth. Continue this slow, deep breathing pattern as you focus on the sound of my voice.  
   
Allow any external noises or distractions to fade away as you gently close your eyes. With each breath you take, feel your body and mind becoming more relaxed and at ease.  
   
Now, imagine a warm, calming light above you. This light represents safety, love, and healing. Feel this light begin to enter your body, starting at the crown of your head. As it slowly moves down, it gently releases any tension or stress you may be holding onto.  
   
Feel the warm light as it continues down through your face, neck, and shoulders, releasing any tension in these areas. Let it continue its journey down your arms, torso, and legs, leaving you feeling completely relaxed and at ease.  
   
As you continue to breathe deeply, imagine yourself in a peaceful and serene place. This could be a beach, a forest, or any location that brings you a sense of peace and tranquility. Notice the sights, sounds, and smells around you.  
   
Now, as you remain in this relaxed and receptive state, listen to the following positive affirmations. Repeat each affirmation silently to yourself as you hear it, truly believing and internalizing the message.  
   
·       	I am in control of my thoughts and actions.  
·       	I have the power to overcome my addiction.  
·       	I choose to focus on healthy and fulfilling relationships.  
·       	I deserve love, respect, and a balanced life.  
·       	Each day, I become stronger and more resilient.  
   
Imagine yourself in the future, having successfully overcome your pornography addiction. See yourself surrounded by healthy relationships, feeling confident, and living a fulfilling life.  
   
Picture yourself making positive choices and engaging in activities that support your mental, emotional, and physical well-being. Allow this vision of your future self to fill you with hope and determination.  
   
As you continue to breathe deeply, let these positive thoughts and feelings sink into your subconscious mind. They will become the foundation of your journey to recovery, providing you with the strength and resilience needed to overcome your addiction.  
   
Now, it's time to bring your awareness back to the present moment. Begin to wiggle your fingers and toes, feeling the energy return to your body.  
   
Take a few deep breaths, and when you're ready, gently open your eyes. As you emerge from this meditation, carry the positive thoughts, feelings, and determination you've cultivated with you throughout your daily life.  
   
**Journal Prompt: Letter to my future Self**  
   
As you continue this healing journey, compassionately explore your past experiences with pornography. 

In doing so, you can create a safe space for self-reflection and growth.  
   
Please take a moment to sit comfortably and remember that your thoughts and feelings are valid. 

This is a non-judgmental space for you to express yourself openly and honestly.  
   
Think back to the early stages of your journey with pornography. What feelings or circumstances led you to turn to porn in the first place? 

Describe the emotions that you experienced at the time and the role that porn played in addressing those emotions.  
   
Reflect on the role of pornography in your life as your addiction developed. What patterns or habits did you notice emerging? 

How did your use of porn evolve over time, and how did it impact your relationships, self-esteem, and daily life?  
   
As you reflect on your experiences as a porn addict, identify any particular moments or situations where you felt especially challenged or confronted by your addiction. 

What emotions or thoughts came up for you during these times, and how did you cope with them?  
   
Consider any past attempts you may have made to overcome your porn addiction.   
What strategies did you try, and what factors contributed to their success or failure? 

What can you learn from these experiences as you move forward in your recovery journey?  
   
Lastly, recognize the strength and resilience it takes to confront your past and make positive changes in your life.  
   
Write a compassionate letter to your past self, acknowledging the struggles you faced and offering words of encouragement, understanding, and hope for the future.  
   
As you complete this journaling exercise, remember that self-compassion and understanding are crucial components of the healing process. 

Thank you for bravely examining your past, and for taking this important step on the path to recovery and personal growth.  
   
And I’ll see you in our next lesson.  
**MODULE 6 It’s Time to Step into Your New Life**  
   
**6.1 Be the Architect of Your New Life**  
   
Heyoo Welcome Back, My Friend\!  
   
You’ve done an amazing amount of work so far, and you should be very proud of yourself.  
   
However, we’re not quite done \- yet.  
   
This lesson is a quick walk-through of the four prompts I want you to journal on.  
   
And like our other prompts, these are questions that I truly want you to “sit with”, do not rush, as this is a critical part of the process.  
   
Imagine yourself as the architect of your new life, begining from a blank slate:  
   
Got it? \- so for the first prompt – I want you to contemplate and write about what outcomes you want in your life 6 months from now? 

In this piece focus on creating a new vision around your sexuality and intimacy for yourself.

Take the time to detail EXACTLY what outcomes you want in your life over the next 6mo.  
   
Why six months?  
Well, it’s short enough to be a manageable time frame, yet long enough to see major changes manifest in your life.  
   
So, ask yourself \- what do you want your life to look like?  
Be Specific\! and as detailed as possible.  
   
Don’t be afraid to completely re-imagine things.  
Don't be held back by what you think is “reasonable.”  
   
Just imagine the place you want to be and how that will look.  
   
For instance \-  
*I want a better relationship with myself as a sexual being.*   
*I want to be sexually excited by my partner again.*  
*I want to have more confidence when talking to women.*   
   
Or *I want love and intimacy back in my relationships*.  
   
You find your words to reflect you longing and desire and expand on it in detail.  
   
Oh \- and this is really important \- Do not write what you DON'T want. Only what you DO want.   
   
Got it? now for our second journal prompt –  
What do you need to do to get there?  
   
So, taking this course was an important first step towards transformation.  
Rejoice, you have already gone through the most difficult part.  
   
Once you are clear about the life you want to live in 6 months, let's determine the individual tasks needed to get you there.  
   
These individual steps, when completed daily will help you put one foot in front of the other.  
   
Let’s identify these new things, when done regularly will manifest your new life, and the experiences you are looking for.  
   
For example \-  
I will have a dinner date with my wife or partner 2 times per week.  
I will speak to at least one woman per day in public.   
I will look at my wife’s sexy pictures daily   
   
Don’t worry all of these instructions are below for you to walk through again\!  
   
When you are completed with the journal prompt let’s move on to the second part.  
   
In my work with people, I've recognized a pattern. Perhaps because I am also a somatic psychotherapist, I noticed often clients were not engaging in their physical bodies; instead, they were stuck in their, stories, thoughts, or heads most of the time. 

Meaning there was little recognition or gratitude towards what they did have, namely “their body”, their breath, their physical agency. Their very own personal Ferrari.  
   
Reconnecting to your body, paying attention to what’s going on inside, and maintaining your connection to it plays a crucial role in keeping the Ferrari in top form.  
   
 To facilitate this, it is vital you create a routine \- a core set of daily practices.  
   
So, your third journal prompt is to write down your core set of daily practices.

Now this may sound incredibly simplistic, but trust me, these things are extremely powerful and compound significantly.   
   
You will include at minimum.  
·  	Drinking water / hydrating yourself  
·  	Exercising  
·  	And voicing out loud gratitude statement  
   
Look,  
Did you know 70% of Americans are chronically dehydrated \- that’s a terrible statistic\! There are tons of advice out there on how much water to drink but start off with 50% of your body weight in ounces.  
   
Complete 30 min of exercise.  
Every day\! It can be anything \- dancing, walking, biking, yoga \- anything \- just MOVE for 30 mins. Take care of your body, show love and appriciation for it. 

As you are exercising repeat to yourself “I love my body, and I need my body”  
   
Every day spending 1-2 min saying out loud the things in your life that you are grateful for, will bring you lasting joy and peace.  

You can do this practice \- either first thing before you get out of bed or right before you fall asleep.  
   
This practice of gratitude takes discipline. But it is such an important self-love exercise.  
   
When done regularly, you will also begin to feel a sense of harmony and balance.  
   
Alrighty, now it’s the time to Say hello to your future self.   
   
In this fourth prompt you will write a letter to your future self.  
Everyone who has gone through it has found it to be an incredibly empowering and inspiring exercise in personal growth.  
   
By envisioning a future where you have successfully left your porn habit behind, you’ve established positive goals to work towards.  
   
This activity also allows you to focus on the benefits of making this change and reinforces your motivation during challenging moments.  
   
Additionally, it encourages self-reflection and promotes a mindset of growth and self-improvement.  
   
So, once you’ve finished your other work, compose this heartfelt letter to your future self, celebrating a life where you have moved beyond the habit of watching porn.   
   
This letter will serve as a reminder of the progress you've made and the person you aspire to become.  
   
To help you get started you can consider these details when crafting your letter:  
   
·  	Describe the positive aspects of your life that have emerged since deciding to move beyond your porn habit.  
   
·  	Explain how your decision to make this change has enhanced your well-being, relationships, and personal growth.  
   
·  	Share your hopes and dreams for this new chapter in your life and the opportunities that are now available to you.  
   
·  	Identify the personal strengths and resources you've discovered or developed throughout this journey.  
   
·  	Reflect on the emotions and feelings you experience as you embrace a life free from the habit of consuming porn.  
   
·  	Offer words of encouragement, gratitude, and wisdom to your future self, as a way to celebrate your growth and stay committed to your personal development.  
   
·  	Remember to be positive, compassionate, and honest with yourself as you write this letter. It’s not only a testament to your determination but also a way to cultivate a sense of pride in your achievements.  
   
Keep this letter safe and revisit it whenever you need a reminder of the progress you've made and the person you are continuing to become.  
   
And again, you’ll find the prompts and clear instructions in the doc below.  
Take your time, and when you’re ready I’ll see you in our next lesson\!

**Module 7: Commit and Walk Away**  
**Outline**                                            
7.1 Take the Oath  
7.2 The Next 3 Weeks  
7.3 The Vitruvian Man  
   
**Module 7: Commit and Walk Away**                                          

## **7.1 Take the Oath**

   
Heyoo\!  
We’re almost in the home stretch. 

You’re NOW aware of the brainwashing that’s been happening, AND you are aware and KNOW you have free will.  
   
You have also created a new vision of yourself and who you’d like to be.  
   
AND you’ve outlined the steps that you need to take to become that person. 

Additionally, you have agreed to the daily accretive inputs that will fortify you against stress.  
   
Now there’s only one last thing to do: It’s time to commit and walk away.   
   
Before you do, let's just check in on a couple of essential points:  
   
Number 1 \- Do you feel CERTAIN that you will walk away for good?   
   
And Number 2 \- Are you filled with excitement for your new path and new life?  
   
OR are you in fear of how “hard’ it will be?   
   
Because this last step is super important:  
   
If you still have doubts about your success, or have anxiety about quitting porn, go back and review modules two through six.  
   
For this to work, you need to feel GOOD about this decision, and you must truly know in your heart that the things we’ve discussed are TRUE.  
   
Review each lesson, thoroughly, until you have internalized them. 

Then, and only then, will you be ready.   
   
If you DO feel certain of your success and have a sense of excitement, anticipation, and positivity for your next path, then it's time to make your commitment and walk away.   
   
I mentioned in Module 1 that I didn’t want you to stop watching porn until the very end. 

This is now your last stop before walking away. 

Make an oath to your future self that you are no longer a porn user NOW 

Mean it with all of your heart, soul and being.  
   
Look at those last images.

Notice how absurd they are.

Notice how extreme and unnatural they look.

Notice all the advertisements on the screen attempting to take money from you.

Notice that the entire page is a trap set up for suckers to make money from their base instincts and feed into their loneliness.

Notice how truly sad of a sight it all really is.

And then Notice that there is no pleasure here.  
   
Just sadness.   
   
And when you finally click that button to close your browser, give a long sigh of relief. Do so knowing that you are FREE.  
   
Revel in that feeling and SMILE.  
   
Imagine the new life you will have and say THANK YOU to yourself.  
Say to yourself, YES\!\!\!\! I AM NO LONGER ADDICTED TO PORN, AND I HAVE NO NEED TO VISIT THIS PLACE AGAIN. I AM HEADED SOMEWHERE BEAUTIFUL.  
                                                                                                                                     
Be self-aware of that feeling for a few days, because on and off, you may notice slight cravings that will pull at you \- this is just the compounding machine, which is used to getting its hit of porn, having to readjust.  
   
Remember \- It does not hurt.

There is no pain.

And It’s only a ‘nagging’ thought \- it’s not a need of any kind.  
   
Your body does not need or even want porn. 

In fact, it is your general anxiety which the craving is attempting to dull through porn.  
   
Instead of thinking about porn \- think about that anxiety. Notice how you feel. Allow yourself to feel those things.  
   
Porn does not have any hold on you \- you have had a hold on IT. 

Relax, let go, and allow yourself the feelings. They will pass quicker than you can imagine.  
   
If you have any doubts \- go back and review the previous modules, look through your journal and the body of work you have produced throughout this course.  
   
This method works when you truly have admitted to yourself that all the things we discussed prior, are absolutely true.  
   
You chose to participate in this course because you wanted to quit porn, and you had a sense that there was a bigger, more beautiful, more interesting, and inspiring life to be lived.  
   
And my friend…..There IS.  
   
If perhaps you’re worried that you have made an oath in the past, and it didn’t work out.  
So what? That's just worry.  
   
That's the very anxiety that you must let go of.

You are alive and breathing.

You have so many things to be grateful for.

You’re good. Don’t worry about what MIGHT be.  
   
Just make the commitment to your new life NOW and feel GOOD about it\! It’s that EASY\!   
   
CONGRATULATIONS\!

Nice work, my friend – on this milestone in your life. I rejoice with you.  
   
See you in the following video.  
---

 

## **7.2 The Next 4 Weeks**

   
Heyooo \- alright let’s talk about the next steps\!  
   
The following few weeks are critical for setting the tone for the rest of your life as a non-porn addict.  
   
And here’s the thing: Quitting porn is, in fact, easy. As we discussed there is zero pain associated with quitting.  
   
That said, what will persist, especially in the next one to four weeks, is that little craving that will kick in because your brain chemistry is becoming used to no longer getting its porn-fix.  
   
It’s important that we have the proper “discussion” with ourselves when we feel this craving.   
   
You essentially have two choices when these cravings kick in.  
   
You can interpret this feeling for exactly what it is: 

a slight nagging sensation due to a chemical brain process that has no hold on your body and is only there because of the brainwashing from society…..Letting you think that you wanted it…  
   
OR

You can decide that this sensation is your body actually NEEDING something that, if you don't get, will cause you to suffer.  
   
Remember, it is you who chooses to suffer when you don't satisfy that craving –  
   
The craving isn’t even that strong and you don’t actually get much out of the whole thing.  
   
There’s nothing actually THERE that will make you suffer.   
   
The proper way to handle this sensation is to recognize it for what it really is.  
   
And the KEY, once you recognize what it is, is to do NOTHING. Just take a couple breaths and relax.  
   
I'll share with you an analogy from Maxwell Maltz:  
   
He says, ’Get this mental picture clearly in your mind, for it can be quite helpful in overcoming the power of external stimuli to disturb you. 

See yourself sitting quietly, letting the phone ring, ignoring its signal, unmoved by its command.  
   
Although you are aware of it, you no longer mind or obey it.  
   
Also, get clear in your mind the fact that the outside signal in itself has no power over you, no power to move you.  
   
In the past, you have obeyed it, and responded to it, purely out of habit. 

You can, if you wish, form a new habit of not responding.   
   
’Also notice that your failure to respond does not consist in doing something, or making an effort, or resisting or fighting, but in doing nothing “  
   
You merely relax, ignore the signal, and let its summons go unheeded.  
   
The telephone ringing is a symbolic analogy to any and every other outside stimulus you might have habitually given control to, and now choose to alter that habit intentionally.’  
                                                                                                 
In short, just because the phone is ringing, doesn’t mean you have to get up and answer it. It does not control you.  
   
It's just noise. Treat it that way.   
   
Having said that, going forward, do nothing in response to the craving.

What you do want to engage in, is your awareness to how you are, or were feeling right before you had the craving.  
   
You’ll find, its usually stress, uncomfortableness, or boredom, precursor signals for us to start becoming aware that there are underlying anxieties at work within us.  
   
THIS is where we want to focus.

THIS is where we will begin to build practices into our lives in order to begin reducing this discomfort.  
   
To “move forward” into your new life means no more “avoiding” or “dulling” these feelings. 

We will acknowledge them, and then we will create practices in our lives that will fortify us from the inside out.  
   
Remember Quitting porn is easy.  
You’ve already done that.  
   
What you’ll now put your energy into is completely separate from that.  
You will put energy into your happiness.  
   
The happier you are, the less stress there is to avoid through developing any type of depletive inputs.                                     	                                            	             
   
And remember, you haven’t given up anything.  
   
On the contrary, you’ve freed yourself and escaped from a sadistic and insidious prison. 

Over the next days and weeks, as you begin to walk down your new path, your health both physically and mentally, will improve.  
   
The highs will appear higher and the lows less low than when you were a miserable porn user.  
   
Whenever you think about porn for the rest of your life, think:                                                         
“YESSSS\! I’M NOT A PORN ADDICT\!\!”  
   
It’s remarkable\!  
Now take some time to process and sit with these new feelings and I’ll find you in our next lesson\!  
---

                                                             
 

### 7.3 The Vitruvian Man

   
Welcome home, my friend\!

Congratulations \- YOU are a non-porn user.

You are free from the trap.

You are no longer brainwashed.

And you will now go and create the life you’ve dreamed of.  
   
But before I get too deep into this lesson, I want to thank you once again for trusting me to be your guide on this long journey.  
   
You’re not quite out of the woods yet, you have some more work to do.

Still, you have done an amazing job thus far and you should be very proud of yourself\!  
   
Now here’s another opportunity you have with your newfound freedom. And that is to help others who are still entrapped in the vicious cycle of porn addiction.   
   
Porn addiction afflicts men disproportionately and thus I designed this course specifically for men.  
   
An important thing to remember, which we discussed in the 2nd Module, is the brainwashing, and how the brainwashing creeps into our minds over time.  
   
One of the most important processes of the brainwashing is that these false ideas and concepts are reinforced through our friends and social groups.  
   
For us men in particular, our views and ideas about sex and porn are heavily influenced by our other male friends.  
   
Since we were young, we’ve been reinforcing “porn watching” to each other. Laughing about it and normalizing it.  
   
We’re grown men now, and while we don’t discuss it as much with our friends and giggle and joke as much as we did, we still reinforce to each other its normalcy.   
   
The irony, however, is that we are no longer adolescents, and watching porn is no longer as innocent of an experience as it once was.  
   
We are harming ourselves and our relationships by continuing to do it \- AND WE KNOW IT. Yet, we do not reveal this to the other men we are friends with.  
   
We conceal this truth.

We think it is embarrassing to discuss.

And it can be.  
   
Rarely we want to discuss our sexual issues with others \- that is a very difficult thing to do. However, let’s imagine for a moment if we did.  
   
There is a saying, “A man is the average of the 5 other men he is in most contact with”.  
   
I firmly believe this.  
   
We tend to rise or sink to the average of the group of people we spend the most time with. 

Now imagine if the way you feel right now, having walked away from porn, and resolved to build yourself through daily practices, was how all your friends felt \- and all of you quit porn.  
   
You would be a part of a group of men with a higher sense of themselves, their values, and their purpose.   
   
Sounds like the circle of men you want to be a part of right?  
Of course, it does\!  
   
And so,  
I want you to tell at least 2 friends, who you care about and trust, that you took this course and how it made you feel.  
   
You don’t need to talk about all your sexual issues or go into detail.  
   
Just explain that you thought you were watching too much porn (which nowadays, just about ALL men can relate to), and you decided to make a modest investment into an online course to help you transform yourself.  
   
Then describe how you feel.  
That’s it.  
   
You’ll find that simple act is incredibly powerful for a couple of reasons.   
   
First, freedom is priceless.  
And if you have truly internalized what you’ve learned in this course, then you know you are free.

Do you want to be the only one to feel that?

Do you want to be the only one who is free?

Do you want to be part of a group of men who are enslaved to porn?  
   
By now, you are probably saying “hell no”. Of course not\!

But freedom comes with responsibility.  
   
Once we obtain the priceless gift of freedom, we must free others.  
That takes some courage and some willingness to lead.  
   
And I believe any attempt to free others, which comes from a place of true understanding, compassion, and belief in a higher purpose, will always be karmically fruitful.  
   
You are free.

And now, you must free others.

Trust me, people will follow you when you exhibit strength.   
   
And second, if it's not enough to know that leading others to freedom is karmically good for you, then understand this.  
   
Your success at creating the life you want, strengthening yourself internally, and staying away from porn, or other depletive inputs, is heavily correlated to who you spend your time with.  
   
So, If you continue to surround yourself with other men with addictions, who are living in the dark, unaware of their own brainwashing, and ignorant of the true power they have \- it will be easier for you to be pulled back into vertigo, and lose yourself into the fog of your mind’s delusions.  
   
But what does that mean?

It means that IT IS IN YOUR BEST INTEREST for your group of friends to see the light as well.   
   
Create a squad of men around you that you will be proud to be a part of, and who will support you in continuing to grow stronger.   
   
Be the Vitruvian Man. 

I believe in you\!

# Quit Porn for Good

## Effortlessly Destroy Bad Habits, Find Your Confidence, and Reclaim Your Sexual Energy

**![][image1]**

**\[Unlock Your Path to Freedom Today\]**

## There’s No Shame in Needing to Change

Have you found yourself: 

* Spending more money than you’d like on porn subscriptions? 

* Wasting hours in front of your phone or computer, clicking on video after video?

* Feeling  more and more lonely, anxious, and even depressed?

* Increasingly unable to connect with your partner sexually and emotionally? 

* Or unable to connect intimately with people in real life? 

Yes? 

**You’re not alone.** 

Excessive pornography use is one of the biggest unspoken struggles affecting people today.  

Men of all ages spend more time on porn sites than on Netflix, which can lead to isolation, depression, and hopelessness. 

Nobody should live like this…

## But Most Methods for Quitting Aren’t Effective 

If you’ve tried to quit porn before, you’ve probably vowed to never log onto your favorite site again and relied on willpower…and then relapsed again and again and again. 

Or maybe you’ve done some research and tried a method or two you’ve found online, only to be given advice that shames you for your usage, tells you to “be more disciplined,” or shows you some gimmicky life hacks that help for about 2 hours.

To continuously relapse because of ineffective and unnecessarily difficult can feel soul crushing.  

But no matter how bad it’s getting–no matter how far this habit has gone, it’s never too late to make a change because…

## There is a Vitruvian Man Inside You 

\[Insert Vitruvian Man image\] 

The Vitruvian Man, a drawing made by Leonardo Da Vinci around 1490 represents the idea that **you come from the same material as the stars in the universe and hold the same potential.**

That’s right; within you is a higher self that *can* be in perfect harmony and alignment. 

Now, this might sound extreme but quitting porn can be the start to unlocking the ideal man within you. 

Consider the “yesses” you just answered to the questions above.

If you could quit porn, and start saying “no” to depression, anxiety, and shame, and instead start saying “yes” to 

* Self mastery

* Self control

* Self confidence 

* An increased sex drive

* Deeper, more intimate connections

You would…

## Improve More Than Just Your Browser History

Porn consumption touches every area of your life, like anything you put in your body and mind.

And just like losing weight by going on a diet, or improving your cardio by quitting cigarettes, managing your porn consumption will improve areas of your life you might not have even known were suffering\! 

| Social Life | Find intimate and social connections more easily, feel comfortable in social situations, and notice an improvement in your self-esteem and confidence.  |
| :---- | :---- |
| **Sex Life** | Become more aroused by your partner, have more more fulfilling sex, have a deeper, more connected relationship with your partner and other people,  |
| **Physical Health** | Understand more clearly what is going on in your body that contributes to your porn habit, lessen premature ejaculation, low libido, and sex drive issues.  |
| **Mental Clarity**  | Regain your time to spend on more productive things, save money for more fulfilling and enriching experiences, find a newfound sense of freedom and possibility  |

Unlocking the Vitruvian Man inside you might sound unattainable, especially if you’ve tried to quit before, have struggled for years, or have employed every self improvement technique out there. 

But the truth is…

## Getting There Can Be *Easy* 

It takes wisdom, courage, and self respect to make the decision to attain your higher self... 

But with the right *process*, quitting porn is actually easy\! 

With **The Vitruvian Man** approach, laid out step-by-step in this course, quitting porn is *simple, effortless, and freeing.* 

This approach doesn’t rely on shame, discipline, or self-control. 

Instead it enables you to envision a better reality; one where you are your highest self, able to create deeper sexual and emotional connections,

It is *the* most effective quitting technique ever invented, combined with a therapist and social worker’s addiction expertise…Take a sneak peek: 

**Module 1: Intro To Vitruvian Man Course**

In this module you’ll be welcomed to your journey, and you’ll find out what the Virtuvian Man is and how you will be unlocking your own Vitruvian Man in the coming weeks. 

**Module 2:The Online Porn Industry**

Let’s take a look at the bigger picture: porn is designed to get you hooked. By understanding the mental manipulation in this industry, you’ll be able to make informed, empowered decisions. 

**Module Three: Your Brain and Body on Porn**

But *why* is it so easy to get hooked on porn? Here we’ll go over the behaviors that turn normal consumption into harmful addiction…and some of the ways to break this stressful cycle. 

**Module 4: Addiction Is Good**

No, this module title isn’t clickbait. Addiction can be one of the most powerful positive forces with the *right* inputs, and I’ll show you exactly how to replace damaging habits with rewarding ones.  

**Module 5: It's All In Your Head**

By understanding *where* and *why* you’re addicted to porn, it’s so much easier to walk away from it. And yes, it really is that easy\! In this module we’ll go over exactly how to do just that. 

**Module 6 It’s Time To Imagine Your New Life**

There is so much more to life without porn\! I’ll give you actionable journal prompts and exercises to become the architect of your new life. 

**Module 7: Commit And Walk Away**		

Congratulations\! In this module we’ll take the final step to quitting porn for good. I’ll arm you with all the tools you need to maintain your freedom and continue living your new life free of porn. 	

**Each module concludes with a guided meditation and journal prompt so that you fully absorb and *live* the material in this course.** 

**\[Get the Course Today\]**

## Testimonials

## Meet Your Instructor

Hello and welcome\! I am Gyasi Bramos Hantman, a New York City native with a lifelong passion for supporting individuals as they journey towards wellness and transformation. I hold a Bachelor's degree in Psychology, a Master's in Social Work, and I am a licensed Social Worker.  
   
Beyond my foundational academic qualifications, I am also certified in Core Energetics, a somatic psychotherapy modality that supports holistic healing and personal development.  
   
Over the years, my work in the field of recovery and wellness has been diverse and comprehensive. I have served as a recovery specialist in various recovery centers in Florida, offering my expertise and compassionate support to those struggling with addiction. My dedication to the cause also led me to contribute scholarly work, including published articles on smoking cessation in the Journal of Medicine.  
   
Subsequently, for a decade, I worked in a non-profit organization in New York City, working closely with men and women diagnosed with severe and persistent mental illnesses, primarily schizophrenia. **These experiences have shaped my approach and deepened my understanding of the resilience of the human spirit.**  
   
**In my private practice, I collaborate with men and women to overcome challenges, implement changes, and embark on transformative journeys to become the best versions of themselves**. My work isn't confined to my practice, though. I also conduct workshops on personal transformation throughout Europe and serve as the Associate Director of the Institute for Core Energetics in Greece.  
   
My approach to therapy and personal growth is a unique blend of traditional and modern concepts, drawing influences from luminaries such as Carl Jung, Abraham Maslow, Gabor Mate, Carlos Castaneda, Rhonda Byrne, Allen Carr, James Clear, and Irvin Yalom. This fusion of influences allows me to offer a well-rounded and holistic approach to trauma, recovery, personal growth, and development.  
   
My specializations include addiction and recovery, mental health, life transformation & coaching, and personal wellness. I am committed to empowering individuals to reclaim their lives from the grip of addiction and experience the freedom of wellness and fulfillment.  
   
**In my online course, I bring all this experience and knowledge to help men quit pornography addiction. It is a safe, compassionate, and professional space designed to foster positive change, healing, and personal growth.**  
   
**I invite you to join me on this transformative journey and look forward to guiding you towards a healthier, happier life.**

## FAQ

**How do I know this course will work for me?**

This course is based on decades of experience working as a Licensed Social Worker with people from all walks of life, as well as using this method to overcome my own addiction. While we can’t guarantee that this course will 100% work for every single person who takes it, we’re confident that if you put in the work and combine it with the knowledge in this course you’ll have everything you need to unlock your Vitruvian Man.

**Is there a money back guarantee?**

Yes\! If you’re unhappy with the course for any reason, just email us info@vitruviancourses.com within 30 days of purchase for a full refund.

**How long will this course take to complete?**

We didn’t design this course to be something you take once and never come back to. We created it as a resource you can come back to over and over again as you progress through different stages in your self-improvement journey.

**Journal Prompts** 

1. Starting something new can bring about feelings of excitement and anticipation. Describe the aspects of this journey that you are most looking forward to and how these elements can contribute to your overall well-being and happiness.  
2. Change can be both challenging and rewarding. Reflect on your resilience and ability to adapt to new situations. How have you successfully navigated change in the past, and how can you draw upon those experiences to stay motivated and positive during this journey?  
3. Reflect on the personal strengths and qualities that you possess which can contribute to your success in overcoming excessive porn use. How can you leverage these strengths to help you stay committed and focused throughout this journey?  
4. Consider the support systems in your life, such as friends, family, or mentors. How have they played a role in helping you face challenges or achieve goals in the past? As you embark on this journey, think about how you can draw upon this support to remain inspired and encouraged along the way.  
5. Setbacks and obstacles are a natural part of any journey. Reflect on a time when you overcame a challenge or setback and what you learned from that experience. How can you apply those lessons to help you stay resilient and optimistic as you navigate this new path**?**

**Journal Prompts**  
   
Pick one and write about it in your journal.  
 

* Reflect on your personal experiences with porn consumption. What emotions or situations typically trigger your desire to watch porn? How has your relationship with porn evolved over time?

  * How has porn impacted your perception of sex and intimacy? Have you noticed any unrealistic expectations or distortions in your understanding of what a healthy sexual relationship should look like?  
      
  * Consider the three misconceptions mentioned in the essay: relaxation, excitement, and sexual satisfaction. How have these beliefs manifested in your own experiences with porn? Can you identify alternative, healthier ways to achieve these feelings?  
      
  * Write about a time when you felt shame, guilt, or self-loathing after watching porn. What emotions did you experience, and how did they affect your self-esteem and self-worth

JOURNAL Prompt 

This module has introduced you to the online porn industry in a way where you can begin to comprehend the magnitude of cultural brainwashing and the signals coming at you.  
 

* Take some time to reflect and write in your journal about your experience growing up and the messages you received about sex and your sexuality, where did they come from, and how they have influenced your experience with porn.   
    
* In this journal prompt, consider the various sources that have influenced your perception of sex and sexuality while growing up. Were these messages coming from family, friends, media, or other sources? How did these influences contribute to your understanding of sex and the role of porn in your life? Reflect on the impact these messages have had on your beliefs and behaviors.  
    
* Reflect on how your exposure to porn has affected your expectations and experiences in romantic or sexual relationships. Have these expectations been realistic or distorted? How has porn influenced the way you communicate and connect with partners? Consider the differences between the portrayals of sex in porn and real-life experiences, and how this contrast may have shaped your relationships.  
    
* Consider the societal factors that contribute to the normalization of porn consumption. How has society's portrayal of sex, masculinity, and femininity contributed to your understanding of pornography? Reflect on the impact of this cultural brainwashing on your personal values and beliefs about sex and relationships. Explore the ways in which you can challenge these societal norms and develop a healthier perspective on sexuality.
